summary: SuperCharts has a genuinely strong indicator FOUNDATION — a 38-indicator tested registry, a working legend/Data Window/manager built on pure reusable helpers, and renderers that already accept rgba fill, lineWidth, dash, log-scale math, and per-channel data — but the UX layer that exposes all of it to TradingView parity is largely unbuilt, so the score lands at 42. The single biggest theme is 'capability built, never wired': scale modes, paneId, line width/style, opacity, percent/log toggles, and a complete plot-cross alert evaluator all EXIST in code yet are unreachable from any control, making most fixes additive wiring rather than new systems. The second theme is a missing settings/discovery surface — there is no tabbed settings modal (M4b), no acronym search (typing 'EMA' returns only Keltner), no colour picker, no legend overflow menu, and sub-panes are a disconnected last-200-bar SVG thumbnail that doesn't share the chart's time axis (the one true blocker, PANES-1). The plan front-loads quick high-confidence wins (alias search, manager identity, legend double-click) and the sub-pane axis blocker, then builds the M4b settings modal as the parent for all style controls and the legend overflow menu as the parent for move-to-pane and create-alert. Hard constraints are respected throughout: every TA fix reuses packages/indicators, the create-alert engine work defaults legacy rows to ma_cross so the 48 live Telegram alerts keep loading, no fix fabricates data (Bollinger source and VWMA use real candles; secondary symbols are deferred and must show no-data), and PulseScript stays an original language surfaced only as data, never cloned syntax.

increments.name: Acronym/alias indicator search
increments.goal: Add an optional aliases?: string[] to IndicatorSpec (registry.ts) and the dialog's Entry union, OR it into matches() in indicators-dialog.tsx (and mirror in indicator-panel.tsx's IndicatorPickerList), and populate high-traffic aliases (ema, bb/bbands, stoch, %r/wpr, kc, dc, sar, st). Fixes the headline 'EMA returns only Keltner' dead-end. Single-source longer text from INDICATOR_LOOKUP[type].description where useful.

increments.name: Browser fast-path: persistent favorites/recents + category rail + saved scripts + row info
increments.goal: Make the Indicators dialog browsable at scale. Keep matching Favorites surfaced during search (drop the if(!lower) guard on the favorites partition); add a left category rail bound to a new activeGroup state that the existing sections useMemo loop respects (keep flatRows/keyboard-nav intact); add a 'My scripts' section sourced from GET /scripts (same endpoint code-terminal uses) that toggles the pane's pulse source via setPulseSource/setPulseEnabled (single-slot per pane — replaces, does not stack); fall back to INDICATOR_LOOKUP[type].description + an info affordance per classic row to surface the stranded registry descriptions.

increments.name: Manager row identity: full-name tooltip + input summary + colour swatch + pane grouping
increments.goal: Fix the 'Ex…' unidentifiable manager rows. In indicator-panel.tsx add title={inst.name||spec.label} to the name span, import and render the existing indicatorInputSummary helper as a muted suffix, add a legendColor swatch next to the eye button (both helpers already power the legend/Data Window, just not imported here), and group the flat list under lightweight 'On price'/per-sub-pane headers by inst.paneId while preserving the flat-array index used by the up/down chevrons.

increments.name: Data Window completeness: per-channel colour, readable plot names, hidden/dim, change bar
increments.goal: Polish the Data Window to TV's per-plot fidelity. Add color to DataWindowChannel resolved via a new exported channelColor(spec,inst,channel) helper (lift the colorFor logic from sub-pane-indicators with an explicit channel→styleKey alias map for ADX/Bollinger mismatches); add optional channelLabels to IndicatorSpec for friendly plot names (MACD/Signal/Histogram, %K/%D, +DI/-DI, Span A, %B…) read in buildDataWindow; carry visible on DataWindowIndicator and dim+EyeOff hidden rows (EyeOff already imported in right-rail); prepend a thin bull/bear colour bar on the Change/Change% rows keyed off o.up.

increments.name: Surface PulseScript + SMC + STS in the Data Window
increments.goal: Make the flagship original features visible in the Data Window. Extend buildDataWindow's inputs additively: stash the latest RunResult.plots in a ref (mirroring indChannelsRef) and emit one DataWindowIndicator per pulse plot; stash the already-computed anchoredVwap.vwap / cvd.cvd frames (currently only written to layer.frame) in a ref and surface them; read stsFrame scalars (already in React state) at index. Gate each behind its enabled flag (pane.pulse.enabled / pane.smc.* / overlays.signalsTrendScore). Read existing per-bar Float64Arrays — never recompute, never duplicate math, never touch PulseScript identifiers.

increments.name: Symbol status line + legend quality-of-life (double-click, collapse, spinner)
increments.goal: Add the institutional top-left reading surface and legend muscle-memory. Render a crosshair-aware SymbolStatusLine sibling above IndicatorLegend in chart-pane fed by candleBufRef.current[legendHoverIdx] (O/H/L/C + abs/percent change, bull/bear classes, reusing formatPrice/formatPercent) — render as a sibling so it shows with zero indicators. Add onDoubleClick={onSettings} to legend rows (+stopPropagation on inner buttons), a local-state collapse chevron, and an optional computing spinner on LegendRow flipped on at recompute start.

increments.name: Tabbed settings modal (M4b) — Inputs / Style / About in a centered dialog
increments.goal: The parent increment for all style controls. Wrap IndicatorEditor's existing fields in the already-present Tabs primitive (components/ui/tabs.tsx): keep the spec.inputs.map loop as the Inputs tab; add a Style tab and an About tab (from spec.description/label); render the whole editor inside components/ui/dialog.tsx keyed off the same editing state, keeping BOTH entry points (rail gear setEditing + legend gear requestIndicatorSettings/indicatorSettingsTarget). A centered modal removes the 'Ex…' truncation. Drive all content from IndicatorSpec — no per-indicator forms; do not rebuild fields.

increments.name: Style tab controls — colour-swatch+opacity, line width/style, precision, defaults
increments.goal: Fill the Style/Properties tab created in INC-7 with real controls (lands inside M4b). Build a ColorSwatch composing popover.tsx + native color input + slider.tsx → rgba, rendered per spec style colour-key (iterate keys like indicator-legend-util's list); add lineWidth stepper + lineStyle enum threaded through chart-pane's overlay push sites (start with the MA/EMA case that passes none) and sub-pane-indicators (dashed:[6,4]/dotted:[2,3]); add a precision style key threading inst.style.precision into formatIndicatorValue (new optional 2nd arg) for legend + Data Window; add a Defaults menu (Save-as-default / Reset-to-defaults) persisting per-type user defaults via the indicator-prefs.ts pattern and merged in onPick. Renderer already consumes rgba (band.fillColor), lineWidth, and dash — no chart-core change.

increments.name: Per-plot enable / show-in-legend toggles (multi-plot visibility)
increments.goal: Give multi-plot indicators (MACD, Bollinger, Ichimoku, ADX) per-plot control. Add an optional plots field to IndicatorInstance keyed channel→{enabled,showInLegend} defaulted from spec.channels (absent flags default enabled, so existing instances are unaffected). Render per-plot checkboxes in the Style tab; skip pushing a disabled channel's line/band in chart-pane's overlay switch; gate buildLegendRows and buildDataWindow on showInLegend. Keep the instance-level eye as the master switch. Defer the per-plot price-line (no horizontal-level primitive in IndicatorsLayer yet) as a stretch.

increments.name: Indicator coverage: DEMA/TEMA reachable, VWMA, input bounds+tooltips, MA offset, Bollinger source
increments.goal: Close registry/math coverage gaps reusing packages/indicators. Add DEMA/TEMA registry+catalog rows (runner cases already exist — zero math); add vwma(values,volumes,length) to ma.ts + runner case reading candle.volume (no fabricated data); backfill min/max/step on all numeric inputs and add optional tooltip to IndicatorInputSpec rendered as help text + onChange clamp (a min-floor in runner.numberInput stops length 0 blanking a series); add an offsetInput()+shiftSeries for displaced MAs; add sourceInput()+source plumbing to Bollinger only (BollingerOptions gains optional source via pricesFromCandles) — NOT to CCI/Williams/MFI/Stochastic (HLC-defined; a source picker there would be misleading).

increments.name: Sub-pane shared time axis + crosshair + real price scale (the blocker)
increments.goal: Make oscillator sub-panes real panes. Capture the live visible range via the already-wired onVisibleRangeChange callback into new subRange state and pass range + barWidth into SubPaneIndicators; replace the slice(-200) tail + i*xScale projection with the canvas's [fromTime,toTime] mapping; pass legendHoverIdx/hoverTime + the indChannelsRef map so SubPaneRow draws a crosshair line and a value-at-cursor readout in its header (read the shared channel map, don't recompute); compute min/max over the visible window and render graduated ticks aligned to the canvas axisWidth gutter instead of hardcoded width=360. Keep the SVG renderer (do not rebuild as a canvas layer).

increments.name: Price-scale modes + axis context menu (log ticks, percent, auto, invert)
increments.goal: Make the declared-but-inert scale modes reachable and correct. Add a log-aware logTicks generator in grid.ts and branch GridLayer/AxisLayer on mode==='log' (hard prerequisite — linear ticks at log positions bunch unusably). Add ChartCore.setPriceScaleMode (sets state.mode, refits, markDirty) + percent/indexed math (rebase against first-visible close) in priceToY/yToPrice; add setAutoFit (extract onDblClick's fit body to share) and setInverted. Detect region:'price-axis' in onContextMenu (reuse the onPointerDown boundary tests) and render a PriceScaleContextMenu (Auto/Log/Percent/Regular/Invert) reusing the MenuItem/MenuSeparator primitives. Persist scaleMode on PaneState (rides the existing saveLayout panes serialization).

increments.name: Legend overflow menu + move-to-pane (paneId becomes live state)
increments.goal: Add the per-indicator '…' overflow menu and make paneId actually drive rendering. Add a 4th hover button to IndicatorLegend opening a popover reusing the MenuItem/MenuSeparator pattern: Bring-to-front/Send-to-back (loop existing reorderIndicator), Settings, Remove, Reset-to-defaults (build explicit defaults from spec — NOT an empty-object patch, which the deep-merging updateIndicator no-ops), and Move-to-pane. Make paneId source-of-truth: give sub indicators unique pane ids at all three add paths + duplicate(), add moveIndicatorToPane store action, group sub-pane-indicators by inst.paneId, and have chart-pane's overlay loop honor paneId==='price'. Add group-focus-within reveal for keyboard a11y. Add-alert item defers to INC-14.

increments.name: Create alert from indicator (M5) — engine + extracted evaluator + form + legend entry
increments.goal: Vertical slice that must land together to be functional. Extract the MT5 SignalRunner's inner evaluateCondition/evaluateConditions/indicatorRefs closures into a pure shared module (no accountId dependency) and import in both signal-runner and alert-engine. Add an 'indicator' discriminant to AlertDefinition.type + IndicatorAlertConfig reusing the SignalCondition union; make alertCreateSchema a discriminated union; branch alert-engine load()/evaluate() AND rowToAlert() with a legacy-default to 'ma_cross' so the 48 live alerts keep loading. Lift IndicatorCompareRow/PriceCrossesRow from signal-builder into a shared condition-editor and add an indicator-alert path to alerts-dialog seeded from a passed IndicatorInstance (copy full inputs → setIndicatorMetadata server-side). Add the legend/overflow 'Add alert' entry (depends on INC-13). NEVER touch the live alerts/Telegram config — open prefilled creation UI only.

increments.name: Chart context menu staples + fixed-status-line OHLC + cursor affordances
increments.goal: Bring the right-click body menu and cursor up to TV staples. In chart-pane store {x,y,price,time} (the price/time are already computed and thrown away) and extend ChartContextMenu with Copy-price, Add-indicator (open dialog), Reset-chart (reuse onResetZoom), and Add-alert-at-price (open the INC-14 creation UI prefilled — never auto-create). Default the cursor-tracking TooltipLayer off (or gate behind a per-pane flag) now that the INC-6 status line shows OHLC, keeping the candle-column highlight by moving it to CrosshairLayer. Set canvas cursor (crosshair/grab/grabbing/ns-resize/ew-resize) from the pointer handlers.

increments.name: Pane resize separators + maximize/collapse
increments.goal: Add draggable sub-pane sizing and focus controls. Add optional paneHeight?/collapsed?/maximized? to IndicatorInstance; render a ~4px drag-handle above each SubPaneRow that calls updateIndicator({paneHeight}) (clamped, dbl-click reset, Arrow-key fallback) and use inst.paneHeight??80 for HEIGHT/svg; add header maximize/collapse buttons (collapsed→header only, maximized→full height, hide existing siblings). paneHeight rides classicIndicators persistence. The price-vs-sub boundary (flexing chart-container) is the heavier stretch since sub-panes are a sibling div below the canvas.

increments.name: Drag-to-add from dialog + legend drag-reorder
increments.goal: Add the DnD interactions on top of the now-live paneId (INC-13). Make dialog rows draggable (onDragStart sets dataTransfer entryId); add onDragOver/onDrop to chart-container resolving the spec via INDICATOR_LOOKUP and calling addIndicator(buildInstance(spec)) — export buildInstance from indicators-dialog so chart-pane can call it. Make legend rows draggable to reorder via the existing reorderIndicator (+ a moveIndicatorTo(index) store action for precise drops). Keep click/Enter toggle and the up/down chevrons as keyboard fallbacks. True overlay-vs-new-pane drop targeting builds on INC-13's reassignable paneId.

increments.name: Interaction feel: momentum pan, eased zoom, magnet crosshair, multi-pane Data Window, source-symbol (deferred)
increments.goal: Lower-priority feel + advanced polish, batched last. Add an optional momentum decay pass to the existing RAF loop (track pointer velocity, decay pan on flick, gated behind a flag so tests stay deterministic); add an opt-in magnet crosshair that snaps to the hovered bar's OHLC (factor findCandleAt out of tooltip.ts into a shared helper). Relax only the active guard so a hovered non-active pane can publish its Data Window snapshot (keep single-writer). Track per-indicator source-symbol (RSI of SPX on BTC) and left/secondary price scale as deferred XL roadmap items — must show no-data for symbols without a live feed, never fabricate candles.

real.title: Search matches only label/description substrings — no acronym/alias search
real.scBehavior: matches(e, group) at indicators-dialog.tsx lines 254-258 tests only e.label.toLowerCase().includes(lower), e.desc.toLowerCase().includes(lower), group.toLowerCase().includes(lower). The Entry union (lines 51-54) has label+desc only, no alias field; grep confirms zero 'alias' references in the file.
real.tvBehavior: Search matches display name plus common aliases/acronyms: EMA→Moving Average Exponential, BB→Bollinger Bands, stoch→Stochastic. Acronym lookup is the primary discovery path.
real.gap: No acronym/alias/synonym matching. Confirmed concrete: EMA's label is 'Exponential MA' and desc 'Exponential moving average' — neither contains the substring 'ema', so typing 'ema' surfaces ONLY Keltner Channels (desc 'EMA ± ATR channels'), not the actual EMA row. Typing 'bb' matches nothing (no label/desc contains 'bb'). 'rsi'/'macd'/'cci' work only because the label literally is the acronym. This is the single most common trader search and it frequently returns zero or wrong results.
real.fixRefined: In apps/web/features/terminal/indicators-dialog.tsx add an optional `aliases?: string[]` to each variant of the `Entry` union (lines 51-54) and OR it into `matches()` (lines 254-258): `|| e.aliases?.some((a) => a.includes(lower))`. Populate aliases on the high-traffic CATALOG entries (ema→['ema','exp'], bollinger→['bb','bbands'], stochastic→['stoch'], williams_r→['wpr','%r'], macd already matches, sma→['sma'], vwap_bands→['bb? no'] etc). Pure additive change to the union + the matches predicate; no rebuild. Optionally single-source the longer text from INDICATOR_LOOKUP[type].description (already imported) so descriptions stay in one place.
real.notes: Verified real and high-impact. The candidate's proposed `('aliases' in e && e.aliases?...)` works but is awkward; cleaner to add `aliases?` to all three Entry variants so the property exists structurally. Severity high is justified — acronym search is core TV parity for the browser dimension.

real.title: No left category sidebar — single long scroll instead of clickable category navigation
real.scBehavior: DialogContent is max-w-2xl (line 325) with one search Input (lines 341-357) and ONE scrolling column at line 361 (max-h-[56vh] overflow-y-auto). The sections useMemo (lines 262-284) stacks Favorites + Recently-used + every CATALOG group top-to-bottom. There are 8 CATALOG groups (~50 entries). No sidebar/activeGroup state exists (grep confirms).
real.tvBehavior: Persistent LEFT category sidebar (Favorites, Personal, Technicals, Financials, Community) lets the user jump to a category; the result pane filters to the selection and works together with search.
real.gap: No category scoping; the user must scroll the whole list or rely on inline uppercase group headers. Discoverability degrades as the catalog grows (saved scripts, more TA).
real.fixRefined: Extend the existing DialogContent into a two-column flex layout: a left rail listing the CATALOG `group` names plus the 'Favorites'/'Recently used' pseudo-groups, backed by a new `activeGroup` state; clicking sets the filter that the existing `sections` useMemo (line 276 `for (const g of CATALOG)`) respects by skipping non-matching groups. Reuse the existing flatRows/keyboard-nav machinery (lines 286-311) unchanged — only the container layout and one guard in the loop change. Do not rebuild row rendering.
real.notes: Verified real. Medium is honest — it's a discoverability/scale gap, not broken. Fix lands correctly in the sections useMemo loop at line 276.

real.title: Saved PulseScripts are not browsable as indicators (no Personal / My scripts section)
real.scBehavior: indicators-dialog.tsx has zero references to pulse/script (grep confirmed NONE). Saved scripts live in code-terminal-dialog.tsx, fetched via api('/scripts') (line 77) and run onto the active pane via the store path setPulseSource/setPulseEnabled/setPulseInput + pulseResults (lines 50-63, 111). The CATALOG is a static built-in-only array. A saved PulseScript study cannot be found or added from the Indicators dialog.
real.tvBehavior: Personal/'My scripts' is a first-class section in the indicator browser; the user's own saved scripts appear alongside built-ins and add the same way.
real.gap: The two indicator systems (built-in TA and user PulseScripts) are siloed in separate dialogs. Real workflow gap for power users who write scripts.
real.fixRefined: Extend indicators-dialog.tsx: on open, GET '/scripts' (same endpoint code-terminal-dialog.tsx line 77 uses) and synthesize Entry rows under a new `kind: 'script'` variant in the Entry union (lines 51-54). On toggle, set the active pane's pulse source via the SAME store actions the code terminal calls (setPulseSource(pane.id, source) + setPulseEnabled(pane.id, true)). Surface it as a leading 'My scripts' section when not searching, like Favorites/Recently-used. Additive — reuses existing endpoint + store, no new backend.
real.notes: Verified real. Caveat: the pulse store is single-slot per pane (pane.pulse.source) — running a saved script overwrites the current pulse, unlike adding multiple built-in instances. The fix should acknowledge this (toggling a script row replaces the pane's pulse source, not stacks), or it risks surprising users. Severity medium is fair. Effort L is right given store/UX wiring.

real.title: No per-indicator details / About / info on rows in the browser
real.scBehavior: Each row renders e.label + a single one-line e.desc (lines 393-405). No expand/info affordance. registry.ts has an optional `description?` field (line 35) populated on only 5 of 28 specs (sma line 49, rvol 303, vwap_bands 326, initial_balance 336, naked_poc 346); the dialog never reads INDICATOR_LOOKUP[type].description — it uses its own inline desc strings. No inputs/author preview.
real.tvBehavior: Hovering/opening an indicator shows a short description/info (what it does, key inputs, sometimes author). Many show a read-more details panel.
real.gap: Minor discoverability gap: a new user gets only one terse line, and the richer registry descriptions (RVOL/VWAP-bands/Naked-POC detail text) are stranded and unused.
real.fixRefined: In indicators-dialog.tsx, for classic entries fall back to INDICATOR_LOOKUP[e.type]?.description (already imported at line 11) when present, else the inline desc; add an info icon next to the Star (lines 407-415) that reveals the full description plus an inputs summary from INDICATOR_LOOKUP[e.type].inputs. Pure extension of the row JSX. Note only the 'classic' Entry variant has a registry `type`; overlay/smc entries have no registry spec so they keep their inline desc.
real.notes: Verified real. Correctly low. One correction to the candidate: it says '5 of 28 specs' — the registry has 33 specs in INDICATOR_REGISTRY (counted), 5 with descriptions; the exact count is cosmetic to the finding. Fix is sound but only applies to classic (registry-backed) rows; overlay/smc rows aren't in the registry.

real.title: No per-indicator source-symbol picker in the browser
real.scBehavior: No symbol/source-symbol control in indicators-dialog.tsx (grep for sourceSymbol/source-symbol returns NONE). buildInstance (lines 153-164) sets inputs from spec defaults + paneId only; no per-instance source-symbol. registry sourceInput() (lines 355-371) only chooses a price field (open/high/low/close/hl2/hlc3/ohlc4), not a different ticker. Every indicator computes on the active pane's symbol.
real.tvBehavior: When adding an indicator the source symbol can be chosen per indicator (e.g. RSI of SPX on a BTC chart).
real.gap: Cross-symbol indicators unavailable. Genuinely lower priority for SC because it touches the data layer (needs secondary-symbol candles aligned to the chart's time index), not a dialog-only change.
real.fixRefined: Deferable and correctly XL: requires an optional `sourceSymbol` on IndicatorInstance, fetching the secondary symbol's candles, and aligning them in the compute path — beyond the dialog. If pursued, add the selector to the future tabbed settings modal (roadmap M4b) gated behind data availability, NOT a browser row. Must NOT fabricate candles for symbols without a live feed (Yahoo/Binance/OANDA only). Recommend tracking as its own roadmap item.
real.notes: Verified real but correctly down-prioritized to low/XL. Risk callout honored: the no-fabrication constraint is real — secondary symbols without a feed must show no-data, never synthetic candles. Should be a roadmap item, not folded into the browser increment.

real.title: Favorites/Recently-used vanish during search instead of staying as a fast path
real.scBehavior: indicator-prefs.ts implements favorites + recent (MAX_RECENT=12, line 11) with localStorage persistence and pure tested transforms (toggleFavorite/pushRecent/sanitize) — this MATCHES TV. But in indicators-dialog.tsx sections (line 264) the Favorites and Recently-used sections are pushed ONLY inside `if (!lower)`, so the instant the user types both pinned sections disappear entirely.
real.tvBehavior: Favorites and Recently-used are persistent first-class sections; users rely on them as the fast path and they stay reachable while exploring (Favorites sorted-first within results).
real.gap: A search hides the user's favorites/recents even when a favorited item matches the query, losing the curated fast-path during search.
real.fixRefined: In the `sections` useMemo (lines 262-284), when searching, still surface matching favorites first — partition flatRows so favorited entries passing matches() render under a 'Favorites' header at the top, or keep the Favorites section filtered by the query. Favorites detection already exists via isFavorite(prefs, entryId(e)) (line 374). A few lines in the sections builder; no data/model change.
real.notes: Verified real. Correctly low/S. The candidate's note that the prefs layer itself matches TV is accurate — the gap is purely the render-time `if (!lower)` guard at line 264.

real.title: No tabbed settings modal — single flat inline editor, no Style/Visibility/Scale/About tabs
real.scBehavior: IndicatorEditor (indicator-panel.tsx lines 222-318) renders one flat `grid grid-cols-2` of every spec.input plus a single hardcoded Color text field. Heading is just `{spec.label} · settings` with a Close ghost button. No tab container anywhere.
real.tvBehavior: TV Settings is a modal split into Inputs / Style / Visibility / Properties(Scale) / About tabs.
real.gap: Everything is one column; no Inputs/Style separation, no Visibility, no Scale/Properties, no About. This is the explicitly-unchecked roadmap item M4b in CLAUDE.md.
real.fixRefined: Extend the `IndicatorEditor` function in apps/web/features/terminal/indicator-panel.tsx (lines 222-318) by wrapping its existing fields in the already-present Tabs primitive (apps/web/components/ui/tabs.tsx). Keep the current `spec.inputs.map(...)` loop as the 'Inputs' tab; add a 'Style' tab driven by spec.channels/spec.style; add an 'About' tab from spec.description+spec.label. Drive all tab content from IndicatorSpec (registry.ts) — no per-indicator forms. Do not rebuild; wrap existing fields.
real.notes: Confirmed real. tabs.tsx already exists in components/ui, so the primitive is in hand. This is the parent of SETTINGS-2/3/4/6 — they land inside the Style tab this creates.

real.title: Color is a raw hex text input, not a swatch + color picker with opacity
real.scBehavior: IndicatorEditor renders Color as `<input type="text" value={String(inst.style.color ?? spec.style.color ?? '#42a5f5')} ... className="... font-mono">` (indicator-panel.tsx lines 304-314). Only the single `color` key is editable; multi-plot style keys (macdColor, middleColor, bandColor, conversionColor, etc.) are not surfaced at all. The observed brief confirms a raw '#2196f3' string and 'No opacity'.
real.tvBehavior: Every plot color is a clickable swatch opening a palette+hue+alpha popover; opacity is first-class on the same control.
real.gap: Hand-typed hex is error-prone and off-brand; no opacity even though the band renderer already accepts rgba fillColor; multi-plot indicators expose only the one primary color.
real.fixRefined: Add a small ColorSwatch component in apps/web/features/terminal/ that composes apps/web/components/ui/popover.tsx (a preset palette + native `<input type="color">` + an opacity Slider from apps/web/components/ui/slider.tsx → rgba). Render one swatch per relevant style key the spec declares (iterate spec.style color-keys, mirroring the legendColor key list in indicator-legend-util.ts) instead of the single hardcoded color field. Store path unchanged: still `onChange({ style: { ...inst.style, [key]: value } })`.
real.notes: Confirmed real. Verified popover.tsx and slider.tsx both exist in components/ui (candidate said 'reuse the existing Radix popover' — it is present). Renderer genuinely consumes rgba via band.fillColor (indicators.ts L87), so opacity is wired-through-able with no chart-core change. Refined the file list to the concrete primitive paths.

real.title: No per-plot line width / line style (solid·dashed·dotted) controls — renderer supports them but UI and most push-sites don't expose them
real.scBehavior: IndicatorsLayer.render consumes line.lineWidth (default 1.5) and line.dash → setLineDash (packages/chart-core/src/layers/indicators.ts lines 102-103). BUT the common MA/EMA/overlay push passes only `{ id, channel, values, color }` (chart-pane.tsx line 1131); supertrend/vwap/IB/poc pass a HARDCODED lineWidth (1.5/1.25/0.75) but never read inst.style.lineWidth. Registry stores a default lineWidth:1.5 on MAs (registry.ts e.g. line 48,60) that the editor never surfaces. No lineStyle/dash field is user-editable anywhere.
real.tvBehavior: Every line plot gets a width stepper + style dropdown (solid/dashed/dotted), applied live.
real.gap: Users can't change thickness or dash even though the rendering primitive accepts both — the data isn't threaded style→chart-pane→layer and no control exists.
real.fixRefined: (1) In the new Style tab (SETTINGS-1) add a `lineWidth` number stepper + `lineStyle` enum (solid/dashed/dotted). (2) In chart-pane.tsx, in the overlay switch (lines 1122-1240), thread `lineWidth: Number(inst.style.lineWidth ?? spec.style.lineWidth ?? 1.5)` and map lineStyle→dash (dashed:[6,4], dotted:[2,3], solid:undefined) into the existing lines.push(...) calls — start with the MA/EMA case at line 1131 which currently passes none. Sub-pane lines (sub-pane-indicators.tsx) need the same thread for parity. Renderer needs no change. Add lineWidth/lineStyle optional defaults on line-type specs in registry.ts.
real.notes: Confirmed real. Minor correction to the candidate: it's not true that ALL push-sites omit lineWidth — supertrend(L1175-76), vwap_bands(L1211), IB(L1218-20) and naked_poc pass hardcoded literals; but none read inst.style and the user-facing MA/EMA case (L1131) passes nothing. Added sub-pane-indicators.tsx to files for full parity (sub-pane oscillators render in a separate React canvas, not IndicatorsLayer).

real.title: No per-plot enable checkbox, price-line toggle, or value-in-status-line toggle in Style
real.scBehavior: Visibility is all-or-nothing per instance: the eye button calls `updateIndicator(pane.id, inst.id, { visible: !inst.visible })` (indicator-panel.tsx lines 104-109). chart-pane's overlay switch always pushes every channel a spec declares; buildLegendRows always reads spec.channels[0]; buildDataWindow maps all spec.channels. IndicatorInstance.style is a flat Record (packages/types/src/chart.ts lines 89-98) with no per-channel structure. No price-line concept for indicator plots exists.
real.tvBehavior: Style lists each plot with an enable checkbox + per-plot price-line + show-value-in-status-line toggles.
real.gap: For multi-plot indicators (MACD, Bollinger, Ichimoku) users can't hide one plot, suppress one value from the legend, or add a per-plot price line.
real.fixRefined: Add an optional `plots` field to IndicatorInstance (packages/types/src/chart.ts) keyed channel→{enabled,priceLine,showInLegend}, defaulted from spec.channels. Render per-plot checkboxes in the Style tab. In chart-pane.tsx overlay switch (lines 1122-1240) skip pushing a line/band whose channel is disabled; in indicator-legend-util.ts buildLegendRows and data-window-util.ts buildDataWindow respect showInLegend. Keep the instance-level `visible` eye as the master switch. Additive — absent flags default enabled, so existing instances are unaffected. (Price-line is the larger sub-part — it needs a new horizontal-line draw in IndicatorsLayer; scope it as a stretch.)
real.notes: Confirmed real. Added data-window-util.ts to files (it also unconditionally lists all channels at line 90-93, so showInLegend should gate it too for consistency). The price-line part has no existing primitive (IndicatorsLayer has no horizontal-level draw), so it is the heaviest sub-part — noted as stretch; the enable/showInLegend toggles are the cheap, high-value core.

real.title: No Defaults menu — cannot Save as default, Reset settings, or save/apply templates
real.scBehavior: IndicatorEditor has only a Close button (indicator-panel.tsx lines 239-241) — no Defaults/Reset UI. onPick seeds every new instance from registry: `inputs: Object.fromEntries(spec.inputs.map((i)=>[i.key,i.default]))` and `style: { ...spec.style }` (lines 76-80). No per-type user-default persistence; indicator_layouts persists active instances, not defaults.
real.tvBehavior: Settings has a Defaults menu: Save as default / Reset settings / Reset to TV defaults, plus named templates.
real.gap: A user who always wants EMA(50) in orange must reconfigure every add; no reset path, no templates.
real.fixRefined: Add a small Defaults menu to the IndicatorEditor header (next to Close, indicator-panel.tsx line 235-242) with 'Save as default' / 'Reset to defaults'. Persist per-type user defaults in localStorage following the exact pattern of indicator-prefs.ts (versioned KEY, sanitize/read/write side-effect wrappers — extend that module or add a sibling). In onPick (lines 70-84) merge saved defaults over spec defaults. 'Reset to defaults' restores spec.inputs/spec.style. Do not touch indicator_layouts or any alert config.
real.notes: Confirmed real. indicator-prefs.ts is exactly the localStorage pattern the candidate cites (KEY 'sc.indicatorPrefs.v1', pure sanitize/toggle + read/write wrappers) — a clean place to extend. No conflict with the protected alerts/Telegram config.

real.title: No per-instance precision / decimals control
real.scBehavior: formatIndicatorValue (indicator-legend-util.ts lines 51-56) picks decimals purely by magnitude (>=100→2, >=1→3, >=0.01→5, else 8) with no override. It is the single formatter used by both buildLegendRows (line 90) and buildDataWindow (data-window-util.ts lines 90-93). No precision key on IndicatorInstance/style; editor exposes no precision field.
real.tvBehavior: Style/Properties lets you set display precision, driving the status-line value and the indicator's price-axis labels.
real.gap: Can't pin an oscillator to 2 decimals or show more precision on a sub-dollar MA — everything is locked to the heuristic.
real.fixRefined: Give formatIndicatorValue an optional `precision?: number` 2nd arg (fall back to the current magnitude heuristic when undefined) in indicator-legend-util.ts. Add an optional `precision` style key set via a number stepper in the Style/Properties tab (indicator-panel.tsx). Thread it through buildLegendRows (pass inst.style.precision) and buildDataWindow so legend + Data Window agree. Additive/back-compatible.
real.notes: Confirmed real. Note formatVolume (data-window-util.ts L42) is separate and should stay magnitude-based; precision should apply only to the indicator-value path, not volume. The candidate's 'price-axis labels' parity is out of scope here (sub-pane axis formatting is elsewhere) — the legend+Data Window thread is the achievable core.

real.title: Settings editor is docked in the right rail, not a movable/centered dialog
real.scBehavior: IndicatorEditor renders inline below the instance list inside the right-rail Ind panel (indicator-panel.tsx lines 175-182, inside IndicatorPanel's div). It's a fixed-width rail element; the observed brief confirms the instance name truncates to 'Ex…'. The legend gear routes here via requestIndicatorSettings → set({ rightRailTab:'ind', indicatorSettingsTarget:id }) (terminal-store.ts line 416).
real.tvBehavior: Settings is a draggable centered modal floating over the chart.
real.gap: Cramped width forces truncation and a single column; no reposition over the live plot.
real.fixRefined: When the tabbed editor (SETTINGS-1) is built, render it inside apps/web/components/ui/dialog.tsx (present in the components library) keyed off the same `editing` state in IndicatorPanel (indicator-panel.tsx line 21,175-182). Keep both entry points working: the rail gear (setEditing) and the legend gear (requestIndicatorSettings/indicatorSettingsTarget effect, lines 35-40) — just have them open the dialog. Keep the rail instance list itself.
real.notes: Confirmed real. dialog.tsx exists. Verified the legend→settings route is exactly terminal-store.ts L416 as cited. Honestly low priority (compounds the missing controls rather than being a control itself). Note TV's full drag-to-reposition is a stretch beyond a centered Radix Dialog; a centered modal already removes the truncation, which is the real pain.

real.title: Source dropdown missing on several indicators; candidate overstates how many can honor one
real.scBehavior: sourceInput() is attached only to sma/ema/wma/hma/dema/tema/rsi/macd/roc (registry.ts lines 46,58,67,75,84/86,98,143 + sourceInput at 355-371). The runner honors inputs.source ONLY for those (runner.ts lines 74-115,145). Many indicators omit it. CRITICAL CHECK: the candidate claims CCI/Williams %R/MFI/Stochastic/Bollinger/Keltner 'math already accepts a source' — that is FALSE: WilliamsROptions/CCIOptions/MFIOptions take only {length} (oscillators.ts 97-164), StochasticOptions has no source, and BollingerOptions/KeltnerOptions (volatility.ts 32,70) take only length/multiplier. CCI/Williams/MFI are definitionally typical-price/high-low studies.
real.tvBehavior: Most TV overlay/oscillator indicators expose a Source dropdown in Inputs.
real.gap: Bollinger and Keltner genuinely have a legitimate, common 'source' variant (TV's Bollinger defaults to close and lets you change it) but offer no Source dropdown; their math does NOT yet accept a source, so it's a math+registry change, not registry-only.
real.fixRefined: Limit the change to where it is defensible. The cheap, correct part: add sourceInput() to the registry entries whose math ALREADY accepts a source (none are missing today — sma/ema/etc already have it), so registry-only coverage is already complete. The real remaining work is Bollinger (and optionally Keltner middle): add an optional `source?: PriceSource` to BollingerOptions in packages/indicators/src/volatility.ts (compute the SMA basis from pricesFromCandles(candles, source) instead of close), thread inputs.source in runner.ts case 'bollinger' (lines 156-167), then add sourceInput() to the bollinger registry entry. Do NOT add a Source dropdown to CCI/Williams/MFI/Stochastic — their math is high/low/typical-price by construction and a source picker there would be fake/misleading.
real.notes: Downgraded medium→low and corrected the scope. The candidate's premise ('add sourceInput where computeAll already accepts a source') is mostly already satisfied — every MA/RSI/MACD/ROC already has it. The only genuinely-missing meaningful case is Bollinger (real source variant), which requires a math change (volatility.ts), so the candidate's 'registry data change only / editor needs no change' is wrong for that case. Adding source to CCI/Williams/MFI/Stochastic would violate the 'never fake values' constraint, so I narrowed the fix to Bollinger only.

real.title: Legend row has no "more" (…) overflow menu — per-indicator actions (move to pane / pin to scale / visual order / source code / reset) all unreachable from the legend
real.scBehavior: Confirmed in apps/web/features/terminal/indicator-legend.tsx lines 36-64: the group-hover span renders exactly three buttons — Eye/EyeOff (onToggleVisible), Settings2 (onSettings), X (onRemove). No MoreHorizontal/… button, no popover. The only MoreHorizontal import in the terminal feature is in left-rail.tsx (unrelated). The menu primitives ChartContextMenu/MenuItem/MenuSeparator do exist in chart-pane.tsx (defined lines 1488/1555/1575, used lines 1512-1543) and are reusable.
real.tvBehavior: On hover, each TV legend row exposes a … overflow menu: Add alert on this indicator, Move to (existing/new pane above/below), Pin to scale (left/right/none), Visual order (bring to front/send to back), Source code, Reset settings, Remove. Primary discoverability surface for per-indicator actions.
real.gap: None of these per-indicator actions are reachable from the legend. Reorder exists only in the right-rail Ind panel via reorderIndicator. IndicatorInstance.paneId exists (packages/types/src/chart.ts line 93) but no UI mutates it, so Move-to-pane is unreachable. No reset-to-defaults, no add-alert-from-indicator, no source/about.
real.fixRefined: Add a 4th hover button (MoreHorizontal from lucide-react) to IndicatorLegend opening a small popover that reuses the MenuItem/MenuSeparator pattern from chart-pane.tsx. Wire the items that already have store actions: Bring to front / Send to back via the EXISTING reorderIndicator(pane.id, id, 'up'|'down') called in a loop to the list edge; Settings via existing onSettings; Remove via existing onRemove. IMPORTANT CORRECTION to the candidate fix: 'Reset to defaults' must NOT be updateIndicator(pane.id,id,{inputs:{},style:{}}) — terminal-store.ts updateIndicator (line 394) DEEP-MERGES inputs/style (inputs:{...i.inputs,...patch.inputs}), so an empty object is a no-op and tuned values survive. Instead build explicit defaults from the spec: const spec=INDICATOR_LOOKUP[inst.type]; updateIndicator(pane.id,id,{inputs:Object.fromEntries(spec.inputs.map(i=>[i.key,i.default])), style:{...spec.style}}) (spec.inputs[].default and spec.style confirmed in packages/indicators/src/registry.ts lines 13/31/33; preserve inst.name so M4a numbering isn't clobbered). Pass pane.id, reorderIndicator, updateIndicator into IndicatorLegend props from the call site (chart-pane.tsx line 1418). Defer Add-alert to M5 and Move-to-pane/Pin-to-scale to M4b/M6 (need new store actions on paneId/scale). Source code should point at PulseScript, NOT clone Pine.
real.notes: Real and high-value. The only error in the candidate was the Reset-to-defaults snippet which would be a silent no-op against the deep-merging updateIndicator; corrected above. Everything else (button count, menu primitives, store actions) verified accurate.

real.title: Double-click a legend row does not open its Settings
real.scBehavior: Confirmed: the row <div> in indicator-legend.tsx (lines 26-31) has no onDoubleClick/onDblClick handler; a repo grep across the terminal feature returns no onDoubleClick. Settings only reachable via the hover gear (onSettings → requestIndicatorSettings in terminal-store.ts line 416, which sets rightRailTab:'ind'+indicatorSettingsTarget). The wiring works today.
real.tvBehavior: Double-clicking anywhere on a TV legend row opens that indicator's Settings — a muscle-memory shortcut not requiring the gear icon.
real.gap: Users expecting TV double-click-to-configure get no response; settings only reachable via the small hover gear.
real.fixRefined: Add onDoubleClick={()=>onSettings(r.id)} to the row <div> in IndicatorLegend (reusing the same onSettings prop the gear already calls → requestIndicatorSettings). Add e.stopPropagation() on the existing inner eye/gear/X buttons' onClick so a fast double-tap on a button doesn't also double-fire the row handler. Tiny, reuses existing path.
real.notes: Verified real and the fix lands correctly. Note: SC routes settings to the right-rail Ind tab (an inline editor), not a movable dialog — TV opens a modal — but that modal upgrade is tracked separately as M4b; the double-click itself is the gap here and the fix is correct. Severity medium is fair.

real.title: No symbol status-line legend (OHLC + change) at top-left of the pane
real.scBehavior: Confirmed. IndicatorLegend (rendered chart-pane.tsx line 1418) renders only indicator rows. The pane header (chart-pane.tsx lines 1394-1411) shows symbol label + interval/chartType badges + last.price + last.change percent, but last is set only from the latest candle / live ticks (setLast at lines 364, 392, 451) — never from the crosshair index — and shows only price + percent (no O/H/L/C, no absolute change). OHLC appears only in (a) the floating crosshair tooltip drawn by layers/tooltip.ts that tracks the cursor (sc-observed.md line 32 confirms this is NOT TV's approach) and (b) the right-rail Data Window. legendHoverIdx (chart-pane.tsx lines 1344-1360) and candleBufRef (full OHLC) are already computed.
real.tvBehavior: TV's top-left status line shows the SYMBOL's own legend row: ticker + O/H/L/C + absolute and percent change for the crosshair bar (latest off-chart), bull/bear coloured, co-located with the indicator legend rows and crosshair-aware.
real.gap: No always-visible, crosshair-aware O/H/L/C + change line co-located with the indicator legend — the institutional default reading position. The cursor-tracking floating tooltip is explicitly not what TV does for OHLC.
real.fixRefined: Render a symbol status row at the TOP of the IndicatorLegend stack (either a new SymbolStatusLine sibling above it in chart-pane.tsx, or a header slot in IndicatorLegend). Feed it candleBufRef.current[legendHoverIdx] (already computed; gate on legendHoverIdx>=0) and reuse formatPrice/formatPercent (already imported at chart-pane.tsx line 71) plus formatIndicatorValue/existing text-bull/text-bear classes used in the header (lines 1407-1408). Pure render of buffered candles — no new data fetch, does not touch or replace the floating tooltip or the header.
real.notes: Verified real. Confirmed the header's last is latest-only (3 setLast sites, none crosshair-indexed) and OHLC lives only in the floating tooltip + Data Window. High severity is justified — this is a core institutional reading surface TV has and SC lacks. Note one nuance: IndicatorLegend currently returns null when rows.length===0 (line 22); if the symbol status row is folded into IndicatorLegend, that early return must change so the status line shows even with zero indicators. Cleaner to render it as a sibling in chart-pane.tsx.

real.title: No loading/computing state on legend rows while an indicator is recomputing
real.scBehavior: Confirmed. buildLegendRows (indicator-legend-util.ts lines 72-95) always emits a value string — a number or formatIndicatorValue(undefined) → '—' (line 52) for missing/NaN/warmup. LegendRow (lines 58-65) has no computing field. Recompute happens synchronously in the chart-pane effect (lines 554-571): it rebuilds indChannelsRef then bumps legendTick (setLegendTick line 569). So '—' during warmup is indistinguishable from a transient empty recompute.
real.tvBehavior: While an indicator is computing (just added, or recomputing after a settings change on a large dataset), TV shows a loading indicator in that row instead of a stale/blank value.
real.gap: A freshly-added or recomputing indicator briefly shows '—' with no in-flight signal; users can't tell 'still loading' from 'no value at this bar'.
real.fixRefined: Add an optional computing?:boolean to LegendRow (indicator-legend-util.ts) and to IndicatorLegend's render: when r.computing, show a small spinner in place of the value (reuse the animate-spin rounded-full border-2 spinner markup already used for the chart loading overlay in chart-pane.tsx lines 1430-1431). Set it true at the start of the lines 554-571 effect before computeAll and clear after indChannelsRef is set/legendTick bumped. Keep additive (default false) so existing tests are unchanged.
real.notes: Real but genuinely low — SC's computeAll is synchronous and fast, so the visible window is essentially one render frame; the practical value is mostly for warmup/large-dataset clarity. The candidate's caveat is correct and severity is honest. Implementation caveat: the recompute effect is synchronous within a single render pass, so naively setting computing=true then false in the same effect body won't produce two paints — to actually show the spinner you'd need to flip it on in a layout-effect before the heavy work or only show it on first-add. Worth noting but doesn't change that the gap is real.

real.title: Legend cannot be collapsed/minimized
real.scBehavior: Confirmed. IndicatorLegend always renders every row in a fixed absolute left-2 top-2 flex column capped at max-w-[62%] (line 24); it returns null only when rows.length===0 (line 22). No collapse toggle, no persisted collapsed flag anywhere (grep for legendCollapsed/collapse in the legend component returns nothing; the only collapse state in the terminal is right-rail.tsx's symbol-category collapse, unrelated).
real.tvBehavior: TV lets the user collapse the legend (a chevron) so a crowded chart isn't obscured top-left; collapsed state hides the rows behind a compact toggle.
real.gap: On a busy chart the legend permanently occupies up to 62% of the top-left width with no minimize, unlike TV. Compounds if the LEGEND-3 symbol status row is added.
real.fixRefined: Add a small chevron toggle at the top of the IndicatorLegend stack backed by local useState (collapsed) — or a per-pane legendCollapsed flag in terminal-store.ts if persistence is wanted — that, when collapsed, renders only a compact pill (e.g. indicator count) and hides the rows. Purely additive; no compute/data impact.
real.notes: Real and accurately described. Nice-to-have; low severity is correct. Local useState is the minimal version (no store change needed) — only touch terminal-store.ts if cross-reload persistence is desired.

real.title: Indicator plot values are not colour-keyed to their plot colour
real.scBehavior: buildDataWindow (data-window-util.ts:86-94) emits ONE color per indicator via legendColor(spec, inst), which walks a fixed priority key list (indicator-legend-util.ts:26-43) and returns only the first matching style key. That single color paints the indicator HEADER swatch (right-rail.tsx:126). Each channel value row (right-rail.tsx:130-135) carries no colour: label is text-muted-foreground, value is text-foreground/90. DataWindowChannel (data-window-util.ts:11-14) has only {label,value}. So for MACD the three rows (macd/signal/histogram) are identical grey.
real.tvBehavior: In TradingView's Data Window each plot's value is colour-keyed to the exact colour it is drawn with (MACD line blue, signal red, histogram its bar colour; Bollinger basis/upper/lower each their own).
real.gap: Multi-plot indicators (MACD, Stochastic, ADX/DMI, Bollinger, Ichimoku, Supertrend, VWAP bands) lose the per-plot colour key. Note the header DOES already show one swatch + the numbered inst.name, so it is not fully ambiguous — hence medium not high.
real.fixRefined: Add `color: string` to DataWindowChannel (data-window-util.ts:11-14) and resolve it per channel inside buildDataWindow's `spec.channels.map(...)` (data-window-util.ts:90-93). Lift the colorFor logic from sub-pane-indicators.tsx:74-77 into an exported pure helper in indicator-legend-util.ts (e.g. `channelColor(spec, inst, channel)`); both call sites reuse it. CRITICAL: the bare `${channel}Color` rule is INSUFFICIENT — verified registry mismatches: ADX channels adx/plusDI/minusDI vs style keys adxColor/plusColor/minusColor (registry.ts:202-204), and Bollinger upper/lower/bandwidth/percentB have NO matching colour key (registry.ts:171,176). So the helper needs an explicit channel→styleKey alias map (plusDI→plusColor, minusDI→minusColor, upper/lower→bandColor, percentB/bandwidth→bandColor/middleColor) plus the existing fallback chain inst.style→spec.style→spec.style.color→default. Then in right-rail.tsx:131-134 render a 2px swatch before each channel label using ch.color.
real.notes: Confirmed in code. legendColor returns single color; DataWindowChannel has no color field; channel rows render colourless. The candidate's own warning about the non-uniform registry key naming is accurate and verified (ADX plusDI→plusColor, Bollinger upper/lower→bandColor only). Downgraded high→medium: the header swatch + numbered names already disambiguate per-INSTANCE; this is per-CHANNEL polish for the handful of multi-plot indicators.

real.title: Channel labels are raw internal keys, not human-readable plot names
real.scBehavior: buildDataWindow (data-window-util.ts:90-92) sets channel label = the raw registry channel key (`label: name`). right-rail.tsx:132 renders it with CSS `capitalize` only. Verified registry keys that surface verbatim: macd/signal/histogram, k/d (stochastic), plusDI/minusDI/adx, spanA/spanB/conversion/base/lagging (ichimoku), percentB/bandwidth (bollinger), upper1/lower1/upper2/lower2 (vwap_bands). camelCase keys (spanA, percentB, plusDI) do not split on `capitalize`, so they read run-together ("SpanA", "PercentB", "PlusDI").
real.tvBehavior: TradingView labels each plot row with a display name: MACD, Signal, Histogram, %K, %D, Leading Span A, +DI, -DI, Upper, Basis, Lower.
real.gap: Plot rows show raw camelCase keys instead of readable names — looks unpolished for a product at institutional quality.
real.fixRefined: Add optional `channelLabels?: Record<string,string>` to IndicatorSpec (registry.ts:22-36) and populate friendly names on the multi-channel specs (macd→MACD, signal→Signal, histogram→Histogram, k→%K, d→%D, plusDI→+DI, minusDI→-DI, spanA→Span A, percentB→%B, etc.). In buildDataWindow read `spec.channelLabels?.[name] ?? name` at data-window-util.ts:91. Registry is the right SSOT since the legend/sub-pane can reuse it. Acceptable fallback: a label map kept local in data-window-util.ts if a registry change is unwanted. No TA/math touched.
real.notes: Confirmed: label is set to the raw channel key and rendered with only `capitalize`. The specific camelCase keys (spanA, percentB, plusDI, etc.) verified present in registry.ts. Fix is metadata-only, respects the 'extend not rebuild' and 'no duplicate math' constraints. Severity medium is honest.

real.title: PulseScript, SMC suite and Signals & Trend Score never appear in the Data Window
real.scBehavior: The Data Window publish effect (chart-pane.tsx:1371-1385) passes ONLY `pane.classicIndicators` to buildDataWindow. PulseScript output (res.plots, computed in chart-pane.tsx:1276-1296, each plot has title + per-bar values index-aligned to bars, palette at line 1280) is rendered to the pulse-script layer but not snapshotted. The 11-indicator SMC suite (chart-pane.tsx:594-662) computes anchoredVwap (AnchoredVwapResult.vwap: Float64Array per bar) and cvd (CvdResult.cvd: Float64Array per bar) — but these go to layer.frame only, not state. STS (chart-pane.tsx:666-750) computes stsFrame held in state (setStsFrame, chart-pane.tsx:116,694) with per-bar Float64Arrays (ema21, rsiSeries, atrSeries, vwapSeries, stLine, adx, diPlus, diMinus). None reach buildDataWindow.
real.tvBehavior: TradingView's Data Window lists EVERY indicator on the chart — including custom Pine scripts — with each plot's value at the crosshair.
real.gap: The two flagship original features (PulseScript, SMC/order-flow) plus STS are invisible in the Data Window — incomplete for exactly the tools that differentiate SuperCharts.
real.fixRefined: Extend buildDataWindow's inputs additively (data-window-util.ts:51-98 signature + loop at 81-95). (a) PulseScript — cheapest: stash the latest RunResult.plots in a ref (mirroring indChannelsRef at chart-pane.tsx:147) inside the pulse effect, pass it as a new optional `pulsePlots` arg, emit one DataWindowIndicator named from res.meta with one channel per plot (value at `index`, colour = the per-plot color resolved at chart-pane.tsx:1282). (b) SMC: surface anchoredVwap.vwap[index] and cvd.cvd[index] as DataWindowIndicator entries — read the EXISTING frames (already arrays), never recompute; note these are computed in a useEffect into layer.frame today, so to read them in the publish effect you must also keep them in a ref/state (small additive change). (c) STS: read stsFrame (already in React state) scalars at `index` (vwap/ema21/rsi/atr/adx + trendDir). Gate each behind its enabled flag (pane.pulse.enabled, pane.smc.*, pane.overlays.signalsTrendScore) so hidden suites add nothing. Reuse @supercharts/indicators / chart-core frames — do not duplicate math. Keep OHLCV + classic path untouched. Do NOT touch the live alert/Telegram config or PulseScript language identifiers.
real.notes: Verified: buildDataWindow call (chart-pane.tsx:1373-1383) passes only pane.classicIndicators. AnchoredVwapResult.vwap, CvdResult.cvd, and SignalsTrendScoreFrame.* are all per-bar Float64Arrays (so index-readable, no recompute needed) — confirmed in anchored-vwap.ts:15-20, cvd.ts:26-29, signals-trend-score.ts:44-71. One refinement to the candidate's fix: SMC anchoredVwap/cvd currently live in a useEffect that writes ONLY layer.frame (chart-pane.tsx:643-656), NOT React state — unlike stsFrame which IS in state. So surfacing SMC requires also stashing those frames in a ref/state, a slightly larger change than the candidate implies (still L, but worth flagging). STS path is genuinely cheap (stsFrame already in state). high is the correct severity.

real.title: No source-price row and no coloured Change bar
real.scBehavior: DataWindowPanel (right-rail.tsx:104-120) renders Open/High/Low/Close/Change/Change %/Volume as plain text rows via the local Row component (right-rail.tsx:97-102). Change and Change % only get a text-colour class (text-bull/text-bear via `sign` at right-rail.tsx:93). No coloured bar element; no source-price row. The `up` flag exists on DataWindowOhlcv (data-window-util.ts:29, set at line 77). Observed brief confirms 'No source-price row, no coloured change bar'.
real.tvBehavior: TradingView shows O/H/L/C then Change and Change% WITH a small coloured up/down bar/indicator for an at-a-glance directional read.
real.gap: Direction conveyed only by subtle 11px text colour; missing TV's coloured-bar affordance. Minor polish, not a data-completeness blocker.
real.fixRefined: In right-rail.tsx extend the Change/Change% rows (lines 117-118) to prepend a thin 2-3px rounded bar span tinted bg-bull/bg-bear keyed off `o.up` (data-window-util.ts:29 already exposes it). The local Row helper (right-rail.tsx:97-102) would need a small optional `bar?: boolean` prop or a dedicated row variant. No new data. Source-price row is lower value (indicators only expose source as an input string) — skip unless trivial.
real.notes: Confirmed: Change/Change% rows are text-only with sign colour; o.up flag present and usable. Pure presentational, no data fabrication. low/polish is correct. Note the candidate's line citation 117-118 is exact.

real.title: Hidden indicators are not visually distinguished — they render as all-dashes
real.scBehavior: buildDataWindow (data-window-util.ts:85): `const series = inst.visible ? channelsByInstance.get(inst.id) : undefined;` — a hidden instance has series=undefined, so every channel formats to '—' (formatIndicatorValue, indicator-legend-util.ts:52). DataWindowIndicator (data-window-util.ts:15-20) does NOT carry `visible`, so right-rail.tsx:124-128 paints the header (name + colour swatch) at full opacity. A hidden EMA is visually identical to a visible-but-warming-up EMA (both: full header + dashes).
real.tvBehavior: TradingView keeps a hidden indicator in the window but dims/marks it off, and shows n/a where a plot has no value — hidden, warming-up, and no-data are distinguishable.
real.gap: Hidden vs warmup/NaN are indistinguishable; the M3 spec intent ('— for hidden/warmup/NaN', per CLAUDE.md) collapses all three to the same look.
real.fixRefined: Carry `visible: boolean` on DataWindowIndicator (data-window-util.ts:15-20); `inst.visible` is already in scope at data-window-util.ts:85 (note: the buildLegendRows path already carries `visible` on LegendRow at indicator-legend-util.ts:58-65, so this just mirrors an existing pattern). real.title: Data Window only ever reflects the active pane; inactive panes show nothing
real.scBehavior: The publish effect is gated `if (!active || rightRailTab !== 'data') return;` (chart-pane.tsx:1372). The store holds a SINGLE `dataWindow: DataWindowSnapshot | null` (terminal-store.ts:188,418-419) keyed by paneId, and DataWindowPanel filters `dataWindow.paneId === activePaneId` (right-rail.tsx:82), blanking to the empty hint (right-rail.tsx:84-91) otherwise. So only the active pane writes, and only when the Data tab is open; hovering a non-active pane in a multi-pane grid updates nothing.
real.tvBehavior: Each TradingView chart's Data Window follows that chart's crosshair; in a multi-chart layout the window tracks wherever the user reads.
real.gap: In multi-pane layouts the Data tab reads stale/empty until the user clicks to activate the hovered pane. This is a DELIBERATE single-writer design (one snapshot keyed by paneId) — a minor UX gap, not a defect.
real.fixRefined: Low priority; must preserve the single-snapshot store (don't have every pane write on every tick). Smallest safe extension: relax ONLY the `active` guard at chart-pane.tsx:1372 to also let the hovered pane publish while `rightRailTab==='data'` (keeping the rightRailTab guard so off-tab panes still skip the work), keying the snapshot by its own paneId; OR activate-on-hover. DataWindowPanel (right-rail.tsx:82) then shows whichever pane most recently published. Keep the active-pane fast path.
real.notes: Confirmed: single dataWindow snapshot in store (terminal-store.ts:188), active+tab gate (chart-pane.tsx:1372), paneId filter in panel (right-rail.tsx:82). The candidate correctly frames this as deliberate single-writer design and keeps it low. Honest assessment — note any fix must avoid every-pane-every-tick store writes, which the candidate calls out.

real.title: Indicator name truncates with no tooltip and no input summary

real.tvBehavior: TradingView's Object Tree / data-window list shows each indicator's full name (recoverable via native tooltip where clipped) and distinguishes instances by their tuned inputs (e.g. "EMA 21" vs "EMA 50").
real.gap: The user cannot tell which indicator a row is, nor read its parameters, from the manager. The tested helper `indicatorInputSummary(spec, inst)` that produces exactly the missing summary already exists in indicator-legend-util.ts (line 10) but is NOT imported into indicator-panel.tsx (verified: no import of it there).

One factual correction to the candidate: the row has FIVE action buttons (up, down, duplicate, gear, trash — lines 116-167), not six; the eye toggle is a separate plain button on the left (line 104). The proposed fix lands in the right symbol (line 110 span) and correctly reuses the existing unused helper. real.title: Manager rows have no colour swatch, so multi-instance/variant rows look identical
real.scBehavior: The manager row in indicator-panel.tsx (lines 103-114) renders only the eye button + truncated name + `spec.pane` Badge — no colour swatch. real.fixRefined: In apps/web/features/terminal/indicator-panel.tsx, near the eye button (lines 104-109), add a small swatch `<span className="h-2.5 w-2.5 shrink-0 rounded-[2px]" style={{ backgroundColor: legendColor(spec, inst) }} />`, importing `legendColor` from './indicator-legend-util' (the same helper the legend and Data Window use). Pure, no duplication. `legendColor` is currently NOT imported into indicator-panel.tsx (verified).

Fix is correct and lands in the right place. Pairs naturally with MANAGER-1 (same file, same import source) — both could be one small commit.
