real.title: No drag-to-reorder; reorder is one-step-at-a-time up/down chevrons only
real.scBehavior: indicator-panel.tsx wires `reorderIndicator(pane.id, inst.id, 'up'|'down')` to ChevronUp/ChevronDown buttons (lines 116-137, disabled at edges via `idx === 0` / `idx === active.length - 1`), backed by `reorderInstances` in indicator-manager-util.ts (line 19) which swaps with the ADJACENT neighbour only. Moving an item across N positions takes N clicks. No drag handle exists, and there is no move-to-index helper (verified: no `moveInstance`/`reorderIndicatorTo`/`targetIndex` anywhere in features/terminal). The store action `reorderIndicator` (terminal-store.ts line 401) only accepts dir 'up'|'down'.
real.tvBehavior: TradingView lets you drag a row in the indicator list to set z-order directly, dropping it anywhere in the stack in one gesture.
real.gap: Reordering many indicators is tedious vs TV's single drag — functional but slower. The up/down buttons are keyboard-accessible (which TV's drag is not), so this is a UX-speed gap, not a missing capability.
real.fixRefined: Extend the existing reorder path, do not rebuild. Keep the up/down buttons as the accessible/menu fallback, and add a drag handle to the row that on drop calls a new store action `reorderIndicatorTo(paneId, instId, targetIndex)` (sibling to `reorderIndicator` at terminal-store.ts line 401), implemented by adding a splice-based `moveInstance(list, id, targetIndex)` to indicator-manager-util.ts (unit-tested alongside the existing `reorderInstances`). Dovetails with planned roadmap M6 (drag-and-drop).
real.notes: Real gap, confirmed in code. CLAUDE.md roadmap M6 explicitly lists drag-and-drop as not-yet-done, so this is genuinely unimplemented. Severity low is honest — the capability exists (reorder works), only the speed/ergonomics differ, and the keyboard buttons must be kept per the project's accessibility mandate. Fix correctly extends rather than replaces.

real.title: Manager is a flat list with no grouping by pane (overlay vs sub-pane)
real.scBehavior: indicator-panel.tsx renders `active.map((inst, idx) => ...)` as one flat list (line 94). Each row shows a small `spec.pane` Badge (`overlay`/sub-pane type, lines 111-113) but rows are not grouped or sectioned by pane. `IndicatorInstance.paneId` exists (packages/types/src/chart.ts line 93; set at add time in indicator-panel.tsx line 75 as `spec.pane === 'overlay' ? 'price' : spec.type`) but is unused for layout/grouping. sc-observed.md line 21 confirms "One row per instance" with no grouping.
real.tvBehavior: TradingView's Object Tree groups indicators by the pane they occupy (main chart vs each lower pane), making it clear which oscillators share a pane and which overlays sit on price.
real.gap: With several overlays plus multiple sub-pane oscillators, the user can't see at a glance which indicators occupy which pane; only a per-row badge hints at it.
real.fixRefined: Group the existing `active` array by `inst.paneId` (or `spec.pane`) before the `.map` at indicator-panel.tsx line 94 and insert lightweight section headers ("On price" / one per sub-pane) above the existing row markup. Pure presentational change reusing data already on the instance (paneId) — no store or math change. Note: reorder (MANAGER-3) operates on the single flat `classicIndicators` array, so grouping is display-only and must not break the existing up/down index logic. Lower priority than MANAGER-1/2.
real.notes: Real gap, confirmed. paneId is populated but never used for layout. Severity low is right — purely organizational, only matters with many indicators. One caveat the fix should respect: the up/down reorder buttons use `idx` against the flat list, so any grouped rendering must keep a stable mapping back to the real array index or the chevrons will misbehave; otherwise the change is safe and additive.

real.title: Oscillator sub-panes don't share the chart's time axis, pan, or zoom (SVG-only, last-200-bars)
real.scBehavior: Sub-pane indicators render as a plain React <svg> in SubPaneRow (sub-pane-indicators.tsx). The x-mapping is a fixed last-200-bars tail: `const tail = candles.slice(-Math.min(candles.length, 200))` (line 46) and `const xScale = tail.length > 1 ? (width - 30) / (tail.length - 1) : 0` (line 71), with bars plotted at `x = i * xScale`. This is entirely independent of the Canvas engine's `timeScale`. The price chart is the Canvas2D ChartCore driven by TimeScale (`timeToX`/`xToTime`/`visibleRange` in scale.ts). The two share no scale, so panning/zooming the canvas (TimeScale.pan / zoomAroundX in scale.ts) does not move the SVG and bars do not align in time.
real.tvBehavior: Each oscillator renders in its own sub-pane that shares the price pane's exact time axis; scroll/pan/zoom move every pane in lockstep and the crosshair lines up across panes.
real.gap: The single most important TV pane property — a shared time axis so an RSI bar sits under its price bar — is absent; the oscillator is a disconnected last-200-bars thumbnail.
real.fixRefined: In chart-pane.tsx, capture the live visible range and pass it to SubPaneIndicators. The ChartCore option `onVisibleRangeChange` is ALREADY wired in the ctor (chart-pane.tsx line 169) and `core.getVisibleRange()` exists (used line 963) — add a `const [subRange, setSubRange] = useState<{fromTime,toTime}>()` updated from inside that existing onVisibleRangeChange callback (it already destructures `{fromTime,toTime}`), seeded once after initial setCandles. Pass `range={subRange}` plus `core.timeScale.state.barWidth` (state.barWidth exists on TimeScaleState, see chart-core.ts:190) into <SubPaneIndicators> (currently line 1479-1483). In sub-pane-indicators.tsx SubPaneRow, replace the slice(-200) tail + `i*xScale` with the same projection the canvas uses: filter candles to [fromTime,toTime] and map `x = (openTime - fromTime)/(toTime - fromTime) * (width-30)` (equivalent to TimeScale.timeToX shifted to the SVG origin). Keep the SVG renderer for v1 (do not rebuild as a canvas layer). Re-render on the existing range-change callback.
real.notes: Verified exactly: tail=slice(-200) at line 46, xScale at line 71. onVisibleRangeChange callback already destructures {fromTime,toTime} (chart-pane line 169); getVisibleRange() exists (chart-core.ts:253). TimeScale.timeToX/xToTime/visibleRange confirmed in scale.ts lines 33/38/48. barWidth on state confirmed. Fix is sound. One nuance: the fix should also account for the canvas axis gutter so the plot width matches — see PANES-5. Blocker severity is justified: without a shared axis the panes are unusable for reading values under a price bar.

real.title: No draggable separator to resize sub-panes (height is hardcoded 80px)
real.scBehavior: SubPaneRow hardcodes `const HEIGHT = 80;` (sub-pane-indicators.tsx line 48) and renders `<svg ... className="block h-[80px] w-full">` (line 85); each sub-pane is a fixed 80px strip stacked in a `<div className="border-t border-border/60 bg-surface/60">` wrapper (line 20). Grep across apps/web/features/terminal for separator/resize-handle/paneHeight/paneRatio confirms NO pane-resize handler exists (only UI <Separator> imports in right-rail.tsx and order-panel.tsx, unrelated). The canvas volume pane is a fixed `volumePaneRatio: 0.18` default in computeGeometry (viewport.ts line 39) with no drag handle.
real.tvBehavior: Every pane boundary has a draggable horizontal separator; dragging resizes the oscillator pane and the price pane reflows. Pane heights persist.
real.gap: Users can't size an oscillator pane; an 80px MACD is unreadable on a tall chart and eats the price pane on a short one.
real.fixRefined: Extend IndicatorInstance (packages/types/src/chart.ts, the interface at line 89) with `paneHeight?: number`. In sub-pane-indicators.tsx render a ~4px drag-handle <div> above each SubPaneRow; on pointer-drag call the existing `updateIndicator(paneId, inst.id, { paneHeight })` store action (terminal-store.ts exposes updateIndicator at line 168/386). Use `inst.paneHeight ?? 80` for HEIGHT and the svg height (replacing the literal 80 at lines 48 and 85). For the canvas volume pane, computeGeometry already accepts `volumePaneRatio` and `axisWidth` (viewport.ts lines 31-32) so thread a draggable ratio through. Add +/- keyboard-fallback buttons in the row header. Persistence: paneHeight rides along in classicIndicators which is already persisted via indicator_layouts.
real.notes: Confirmed: HEIGHT=80 (line 48), h-[80px] (line 85). No resize handler anywhere in terminal. updateIndicator action confirmed in terminal-store.ts. volumePaneRatio/axisWidth are computeGeometry params (viewport.ts 31-32,36,39). IndicatorInstance is the correct type to extend (chart.ts line 89). Fix lands correctly.

real.title: No move-to-pane / move-to-new-pane action; paneId is computed once and ignored
real.scBehavior: IndicatorInstance carries `paneId: 'price' | string` (chart.ts line 93) but it is assigned once at add-time: `paneId: spec.pane === 'overlay' ? 'price' : spec.type` in indicator-panel.tsx (line 75) AND indicators-dialog.tsx (line 158). No renderer reads it: the overlay path (IndicatorsLayer feed, chart-pane.tsx ~line 1112) keys off `spec.pane !== 'overlay'`, and SubPaneIndicators filters by `INDICATOR_LOOKUP[i.type]?.pane === 'sub'` (sub-pane-indicators.tsx line 15) — never by inst.paneId. There is no moveIndicatorToPane store action (grep confirms none). Because every sub indicator of a type gets paneId=spec.type, two of the same type collide on one id. The legend controls are eye/gear/× only (indicator-legend.tsx lines 41/50/59) and the Ind panel is eye/up/down/duplicate/gear/trash (indicator-panel.tsx) — no Move-to-pane.
real.tvBehavior: An indicator's '...' menu offers Move to → existing pane / new pane above / below; overlays merge onto price or split into their own pane; pane assignment is real state the renderer honors.
real.gap: A user can't pull an EMA off price into its own pane, stack two oscillators into one pane, or reorder which pane an oscillator lives in.
real.fixRefined: Make paneId the source of truth instead of spec.type. (1) On add, give sub indicators a unique pane id so duplicates don't collide — note duplicate() in indicator-panel.tsx (line 44) already generates a fresh INSTANCE id via nanoid but copies the colliding paneId, so fix both the add paths (indicator-panel.tsx:75 and indicators-dialog.tsx:158) and duplicate(). (2) Add `moveIndicatorToPane(paneId, instId, target)` to terminal-store.ts setting inst.paneId. (3) In SubPaneIndicators, group visible sub indicators by inst.paneId (multiple sharing one paneId draw in one box) instead of one box per instance. (4) Add a 'Move to…' item to the legend controls (indicator-legend.tsx, which currently only has onToggleVisible/onSettings/onRemove props) and the Ind row (indicator-panel.tsx), listing price + each existing sub-pane + 'New pane'. Honor the overlay/sub flip: moved-to-price feeds the IndicatorsLayer path, otherwise the SVG sub-pane path. Reuse updateIndicator plumbing.
real.notes: Confirmed paneId is dead state: assigned at indicator-panel.tsx:75, indicators-dialog.tsx:158, strategy-builder-dialog.tsx (several), never read by any renderer. Correction to candidate: the candidate omitted indicators-dialog.tsx:158 as a third add path and strategy-builder-dialog.tsx which also sets paneId — those must be updated too for consistency. Also the existing duplicate() in indicator-panel.tsx already gives a fresh instance id but reuses the spec.type-derived paneId, so the collision is real. Otherwise accurate. High severity justified.

real.title: No maximize / restore / collapse / hide controls per sub-pane
real.scBehavior: SubPaneRow's header (sub-pane-indicators.tsx lines 80-84) renders only `{spec.label}` + `{paramsLabel(inst)}` — no buttons. No collapse/maximize flags exist on IndicatorInstance (chart.ts line 89 has only id/type/name/paneId/inputs/style/visible/locked) or on any PaneState. The only related controls are the on-chart legend eye/gear/× (indicator-legend.tsx) and the Ind panel eye/up/down/duplicate/gear/trash (indicator-panel.tsx) — these toggle inst.visible, not a pane maximize/collapse.
real.tvBehavior: Each pane has hover controls to maximize to full height (and restore), collapse to a thin strip, hide, or remove — letting a trader focus or fold an oscillator without deleting it.
real.gap: A user can't focus or fold an oscillator pane; the only options are full delete or the fixed 80px strip.
real.fixRefined: Add a small control cluster to the SubPaneRow header flex div (lines 80-84). Back it with two optional fields on IndicatorInstance (chart.ts line 89): `collapsed?: boolean` and `maximized?: boolean`, mutated via the existing updateIndicator action (terminal-store.ts:386). Collapsed → render header only (skip the <svg> at line 85); maximized → in SubPaneIndicators give that row the full stacked height and skip rendering siblings. Hide already exists as the visible eye. Purely additive UI + two boolean flags; reuse updateIndicator, no new pane subsystem.
real.notes: Confirmed header has no controls (lines 80-84). No collapsed/maximized on IndicatorInstance. Fix is additive and lands correctly. Medium severity is honest — this is a focus/ergonomics nicety, not a core blocker; effort M is generous (closer to S since it's two flags + header buttons), but maximized interaction with sibling layout adds a little, so M is acceptable.

real.title: Sub-pane has no real price scale / right-axis aligned to the price pane's axis gutter
real.scBehavior: SubPaneRow computes local min/max over the last-200 tail (lines 50-70) and draws exactly two <text> labels: `{max.toFixed(2)}` at x={width-28} y=12 (line 126) and `{min.toFixed(2)}` at the bottom (line 127). width is the hardcoded constant 360 passed from chart-pane.tsx (`<SubPaneIndicators ... width={360} />`, line 1482), while the canvas axis gutter is `axisWidth=64` (viewport.ts line 36). So the right edges don't line up and there are no graduated ticks. Reference bands draw via BandRefs (lines 159-197) as dashed lines but carry no axis labels.
real.tvBehavior: Every pane has its own price scale in the same right-hand axis column as the price pane, with auto-fit ticks and labeled reference levels (e.g. RSI 70/30).
real.gap: The sub-pane value axis is a two-number approximation that neither auto-fits to the visible window (fixed 200-bar tail) nor aligns to the price axis column, so panes look mismatched and values are hard to read.
real.fixRefined: Once PANES-1 makes the sub-pane range-driven, compute min/max over the VISIBLE window (not slice(-200)) and render 3-4 graduated tick labels via a small shared helper. Pass the real measured pane width and the canvas axisWidth (64, from computeGeometry default in viewport.ts:36) down from chart-pane.tsx instead of the hardcoded 360 (line 1482), and reserve that same right gutter so the sub-pane axis lines up with the price axis. Label the BandRefs levels at the axis. Pure SVG changes in sub-pane-indicators.tsx plus passing two numbers down; no engine rewrite.
real.notes: Confirmed: two text labels (lines 126-127) at width-28, hardcoded width={360} (chart-pane line 1482), axisWidth default 64 (viewport.ts:36). BandRefs draws unlabeled dashed lines (159-197). Fix is correct and rightly depends on PANES-1. Medium severity honest. Note: width=360 is doubly wrong because the SVG uses viewBox so it's not even the real rendered width — passing a measured width is the right call.

real.title: Crosshair and per-bar value readout don't extend into the oscillator sub-pane
real.scBehavior: The crosshair is a Canvas layer (CrosshairLayer, registered in chart-core.ts:298) bounded to the canvas geometry: it draws the vertical line from `geometry.pricePane.y` down to `pricePane.height + volumePane.height` (crosshair.ts lines 21-24), i.e. price+volume panes only. The oscillator SVG is a separate DOM element below the canvas (chart-pane.tsx line 1479), so the crosshair line stops at the canvas bottom and never crosses the sub-pane. SubPaneRow's header shows only static paramsLabel (line 83), not the crosshair-bar value — even though chart-pane.tsx already tracks hoverTime (line 149), computes legendHoverIdx (line 1344), and holds per-indicator channel arrays in indChannelsRef.current keyed by inst.id (line 147, filled at lines 554-568). SubPaneIndicators receives none of that (only candles/indicators/width, line 1479-1483).
real.tvBehavior: Moving the crosshair draws a vertical line through the price pane AND every sub-pane, and each pane's legend shows that bar's value (e.g. RSI=62.4) at the crosshair time.
real.gap: The oscillator pane is non-interactive: no crosshair line through it and no value-at-crosshair, so a trader can't read RSI under the cursor in its own pane (only in the top-left legend / Data tab).
real.fixRefined: Pass `legendHoverIdx`/`hoverTime` (already computed in chart-pane.tsx, lines 149/1344) and the `indChannelsRef.current` map (line 147) into <SubPaneIndicators> (line 1479). In SubPaneRow, draw a vertical <line> at the crosshair x (computed from the shared range from PANES-1) and print the channel value(s) at legendHoverIdx in the row header. Read from the passed channel map keyed by inst.id rather than recomputing in SubPaneRow's local `computeAll` (lines 38-43) so the readout matches the top-left legend exactly. SVG-only addition reusing existing computed state.
real.notes: Confirmed: CrosshairLayer bounded to pricePane+volumePane (crosshair.ts lines 14,21-24). SubPaneRow header static (line 83). hoverTime/legendHoverIdx/indChannelsRef all exist in chart-pane and are NOT passed to SubPaneIndicators (props are only candles/indicators/width). One refinement worth noting: SubPaneRow currently recomputes channels itself via computeAll (lines 38-43), so passing indChannelsRef avoids double-compute. Fix correct, depends on PANES-1 for the x mapping. Medium severity honest.

real.title: No way to switch price-scale mode (Log / Percent / Indexed-to-100 / Auto / Regular) — modes declared but inert
real.scBehavior: PriceScale (packages/chart-core/src/scale.ts:77) declares PriceScaleMode = 'linear' | 'log' | 'percent' and priceToY/yToPrice implement a real log branch (scale.ts:100-107, 116-121). But mode is hardcoded 'linear' at construction (chart-core.ts:118) and there is NO public method on ChartCore to change it — a repo-wide grep for setPriceScaleMode/setScaleMode/setMode/state.mode= returns nothing outside scale.ts's type decl. PaneState (terminal-store.ts:56-125) has no scaleMode field; togglePaneOverlay/setStsSetting exist but nothing for scale mode. 'percent' is NOT implemented — priceToY only branches on 'log', so 'percent' silently uses linear math. Indexed-to-100 has no representation at all.
real.tvBehavior: TradingView exposes Auto, Logarithmic, Percent, Indexed-to-100 and Regular scale modes, toggled from buttons near the axis and from the price-scale right-click menu. Log re-maps the axis; Percent re-bases to % off the leftmost visible bar; Indexed-to-100 normalizes to 100.
real.gap: Users cannot enable log scale (essential for long-range crypto spanning 10x+) or percent/indexed comparison scaling, despite the log math already existing. The capability is one setter + one toggle away but completely unreachable.
real.fixRefined: Add ChartCore.setPriceScaleMode(mode: PriceScaleMode) that sets this.priceScale.state.mode, then calls fitPriceScaleToVisible() (chart-core.ts:531) + markDirty(). Add a scaleMode: PriceScaleMode field to PaneState (terminal-store.ts:56) with a setScaleMode(paneId, mode) action — it WILL persist, since saveLayout serializes config:{layoutId,layout,panes} (terminal-top-bar.tsx:128). Wire from the new price-scale menu (SCALE-3) and re-apply on chart-pane mount. For 'percent'/indexed, extend priceToY/yToPrice to subtract/normalize against a baseValue (first visible candle close) captured by the SAME visible-range scan in fitPriceScaleToVisible (chart-core.ts:535-539) — do not duplicate any TA/math, reuse @supercharts/indicators for nothing new here (pure pixel transform).
real.notes: Confirmed across all 4 files. Persistence claim verified: panes array is serialized whole in saveLayout (terminal-top-bar.tsx:128), so a new PaneState field round-trips. Honest caveat: percent/indexed-to-100 are genuinely new math (priceToY currently only special-cases 'log'), so the 'log already works' framing applies only to log; percent/indexed are net-new but still small.

real.title: Log-mode gridlines and axis labels computed in linear space → wrong, bunched, non-round ticks
real.scBehavior: GridLayer.render (grid.ts:15) and AxisLayer.render (axis.ts:21) both call niceTicks(priceScale.state.priceMin, priceScale.state.priceMax, 8). niceTicks (grid.ts:52-61) generates LINEARLY-spaced values (start = ceil(min/step)*step, then v += step), then each is plotted via priceScale.priceToY(p). In log mode priceToY applies log spacing (scale.ts:100-105), so those linearly-spaced values land at log positions — they bunch toward one end and are not log-round. So the moment log mode (SCALE-1) is reachable, the axis/grid render unusably.
real.tvBehavior: In log mode TradingView draws round, log-appropriate tick values (1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50…) that are evenly spaced on screen because spacing follows the log mapping.
real.gap: Log scale, once enabled, renders an unusable axis: ticks crowd at one end with arbitrary values. This is a hard dependency that blocks SCALE-1 from being shippable.
real.fixRefined: Add a log-aware generator (e.g. logTicks(min, max, target)) next to niceTicks in grid.ts that walks decades (powers of 10) and emits 1/2/5 mantissa steps within the visible decade(s). In BOTH GridLayer.render (grid.ts:15) and AxisLayer.render (axis.ts:21), branch on priceScale.state.mode === 'log' to pick logTicks vs niceTicks. Leave niceTicks untouched for linear/percent — extend, don't replace. AxisLayer imports niceTicks from './grid' already (axis.ts:2) so export logTicks there too.

real.title: Right-click on the price axis shows the generic chart menu, not a dedicated price-scale menu
real.scBehavior: ChartCore.onContextMenu (chart-core.ts:474-491) fires a single contextmenu ChartPointerEvent for any right-click and does NOT indicate the region (price axis vs body vs time axis); ChartPointerEvent (chart-core.ts:41-52) has no region field. chart-pane.tsx:159-160 unconditionally sets menu={x,y} on any contextmenu, and the menu render (chart-pane.tsx:1447) always mounts ChartContextMenu (chart-pane.tsx:1488), whose items are Reset zoom / Delete drawing / heatmap / volume profile / deep trades / footprint / volume pane — purely body/overlay. Brief confirms the axis shows the SAME generic menu with no scale menu.
real.tvBehavior: Right-clicking the price scale opens a scale-specific menu: Auto, Logarithmic, Percent, Indexed-to-100, Regular, Lock price to bar ratio, Invert scale, Scale settings.

In chart-pane.tsx store region alongside the menu state (line 115: setMenu now {x,y,region}); when region==='price-axis' render a new PriceScaleContextMenu reusing the existing MenuItem/MenuSeparator primitives (chart-pane.tsx:1555-1577) with items Auto / Logarithmic / Percent / Indexed-to-100 / Regular / Invert / Scale settings, each calling the SCALE-1/4/5 setters.

real.title: Auto-fit cannot be re-enabled except via an undiscoverable double-click; no explicit Auto control or state indicator
real.scBehavior: ChartCore tracks a private field autoFollow (chart-core.ts:82, default true), set false on wheel-zoom (chart-core.ts:383), pan (chart-core.ts:409) and implicitly during axis drag; only set true again inside onDblClick (chart-core.ts:459). There is no public getter/setter and no UI affordance — the only way to restore auto-fit is to know the double-click gesture. Reset-zoom in the body menu just dispatches a synthetic dblclick (chart-pane.tsx:1453-1457).
real.tvBehavior: TradingView shows an 'Auto' button on the price scale, highlighted when auto-fit is on; any manual zoom/pan flips it off and it can be re-enabled from the button or the scale menu.
real.gap: After any manual zoom the price scale stays frozen with no discoverable way back to auto-fit other than an unlabelled double-click, and no indication of which mode is active.
real.fixRefined: Expose ChartCore.setAutoFit(on: boolean) and getAutoFit(): both read/write the existing private autoFollow field (chart-core.ts:82) — when turning on, run the same fit branch as onDblClick (chart-core.ts:457-472: reset rightTime + pxPerMs + fitPriceScaleToVisible + scheduleRangeChange + markDirty), ideally by extracting that body into a private autoFit() and calling it from both. Add an 'Auto' entry to the price-scale menu (SCALE-3). Reuses existing state — no new fit logic.
real.notes: Real. One correction to the candidate: the internal field is autoFollow (not autoFit), and it is private; the setter must read/write that exact field. Recommend extracting onDblClick's body into a reusable private method so setAutoFit(true) and the existing dblclick share one implementation rather than duplicating the fit math. Severity medium is fair — there IS a working escape hatch (dblclick), it is just undiscoverable.

real.title: Invert scale exists in state but is unreachable; 'Lock price to bar ratio' is missing
real.scBehavior: PriceScaleState.inverted is fully wired in priceToY/yToPrice (scale.ts:87, 98, 109, 113-114: return inverted ? height - y : y, and effY = inverted ? height - y : y) but is hardcoded false at construction (chart-core.ts:119) with no setter and no UI. real.title: Only a single right-side price scale; no left scale / multiple scales

real.title: Cannot drag an indicator from the dialog onto the chart (no overlay-vs-new-pane drop zones)
real.scBehavior: Each catalog row in indicators-dialog.tsx is a `<div role="button" tabIndex={0} onClick={() => toggle(e)}>` (lines 377-392). `toggle` (217-234) immediately mutates store via togglePaneOverlay/toggleSmcOverlay or addIndicator(pane.id, buildInstance(spec)). `buildInstance` (153-164) hardcodes `paneId: spec.pane === 'overlay' ? 'price' : spec.type`. No draggable/onDragStart/dataTransfer on rows; chart-container wrapper (chart-pane.tsx:1412) has no onDrop/onDragOver. Repo-wide grep found zero drag/drop handlers in apps/web/features/terminal and no DnD library in apps/web/package.json.
real.tvBehavior: In TradingView you grab an indicator row in the picker and drag it onto the chart; dropping on the price pane adds it as an overlay, dropping on a sub-pane or the highlighted 'new pane' zone creates/targets that pane.
real.gap: There is no drag-from-dialog interaction at all, so no overlay-vs-pane drop-zone semantics. Placement (overlay vs sub) is dictated entirely by the indicator's spec via buildInstance, never by where the user drops.
real.fixRefined: EXTEND, don't rebuild. In indicators-dialog.tsx make each row draggable with onDragStart setting dataTransfer 'application/x-sc-indicator' to entryId(e) (entryId exists at line 36; INDICATOR_LOOKUP already imported, line 11). In chart-pane.tsx's data-testid=chart-container div (line 1412) add onDragOver (preventDefault + highlight) and onDrop that reads the id, resolves the spec via INDICATOR_LOOKUP, and calls the existing addIndicator(pane.id, buildInstance(spec)). The overlay-vs-sub drop targeting only becomes meaningful once DND-2/DND-3 make paneId reassignable; on its own this delivers 'drop-to-add', not full TV drop-zone targeting.
real.notes: All concrete claims verified. Two refinements vs the candidate: (1) buildInstance is module-local in indicators-dialog.tsx, so chart-pane cannot call it without an export; (2) downgraded the implied 'full drop-zone semantics' to 'drop-to-add' because true overlay-vs-new-pane targeting depends on DND-2/DND-3 landing first (paneId is currently dead at render time). Severity high kept.

real.title: No 'move to pane' for indicators - paneId is set at creation and never reassignable
real.scBehavior: IndicatorInstance.paneId (packages/types/src/chart.ts:93) is assigned once in buildInstance (indicators-dialog.tsx:158) and indicator-panel.tsx:75, then never updated - grep for setIndicatorPane/moveIndicatorTo across apps/web + packages returns nothing. paneId is DEAD in the render pipeline: the overlay draw loop in chart-pane.tsx:1112-1115 filters by `spec.pane !== 'overlay'`, and sub-pane-indicators.tsx:15 filters by `INDICATOR_LOOKUP[i.type]?.pane === 'sub'` - neither reads inst.paneId. Change BOTH render paths to key off inst.paneId - group sub-pane-indicators.tsx by inst.paneId (instead of only spec.pane==='sub') AND have chart-pane.tsx's overlay loop (line 1112) include any inst whose paneId==='price' even when spec.pane==='sub' (and vice versa). Surface as a menu to satisfy the keyboard/menu fallback: add a 'Move to' control to indicator-legend.tsx's hover cluster and/or indicator-panel.tsx's per-row button group (lines 115-168).
real.notes: Verified real; diagnosis accurate - paneId is genuinely never reassigned and never read at render time. Refinement: the candidate listed only sub-pane-indicators.tsx for the grouping change, but chart-pane.tsx's overlay draw loop (line 1112) ALSO filters by spec.pane and must change for a true move-between-panes (e.g. pulling an oscillator onto price); added chart-pane.tsx to files. Severity high kept - a core TV pane-organization affordance is entirely absent.

real.title: Sub-panes are fixed-height SVG rows with no draggable separator to resize
real.scBehavior: sub-pane-indicators.tsx renders each sub indicator as a stacked SubPaneRow with `const HEIGHT = 80` (line 48) inside `<svg viewBox="0 0 ${width} ${HEIGHT}" className="block h-[80px] w-full">` (line 85). No separator element, no onMouseDown/cursor-row-resize handle, no per-pane height state; container is just `<div className="border-t border-border/60 ...">` (line 20). chart-pane.tsx places <SubPaneIndicators candles=... indicators=... width={360}/> (lines 1479-1483) below the canvas with no resize boundary. Grep found no resize handlers anywhere in the terminal feature.
real.tvBehavior: TradingView draws a draggable horizontal separator between the price pane and each indicator pane (and between stacked indicator panes); dragging grows one and shrinks the neighbor, double-click resets.
real.gap: Indicator sub-panes cannot be resized; they are locked at 80px regardless of content, and the price pane can't be grown to give an oscillator more room. The separator-drag interaction TV users rely on does not exist.
real.fixRefined: EXTEND sub-pane-indicators.tsx: replace `const HEIGHT = 80` (line 48) with a per-instance height read from a new store field paneHeights: Record<string,number> (keyed by inst.id) defaulting to 80, and render a thin separator div above each SubPaneRow with onMouseDown tracking pointer delta -> new setPaneHeight store action (clamped, double-click reset). The SVG already uses viewBox + a dynamic yScale (lines 71-73), so it rescales cleanly. Add a focusable separator with Arrow-key nudge for the no-mouse fallback. No chart-core change. CAVEAT: SC's sub-panes live in a separate div BELOW the canvas (chart-pane.tsx:1479, outside the chart-container at 1412); resizing sub-pane rows is straightforward, but 'grow the price pane vs the sub-pane strip' additionally needs to flex the chart-container height (the canvas auto-sizes via absolute inset-0), which is a bigger lift.
real.notes: Verified real - HEIGHT=80 hardcoded, fixed 80px SVG, zero resize handlers. Added caveat: resizing sub-pane ROWS is easy, but the price-vs-sub boundary the candidate emphasizes is structurally harder because sub-panes are a sibling div outside the canvas-backed chart-container and the canvas resizes via absolute inset-0 to its parent. Severity high kept: zero resize capability is a real gap vs TV.

Reordering exists ONLY in the right-rail IndicatorPanel via ChevronUp/ChevronDown calling reorderIndicator(pane.id, inst.id, 'up'|'down') (indicator-panel.tsx:121-137 -> store reorderIndicator at terminal-store.ts:401-406 -> reorderInstances on classicIndicators[]). Also mirror the existing up/down chevrons into the legend's hover cluster (lines 36-64) for a no-drag path. real.scBehavior: Reorder HAS a keyboard fallback: indicator-panel.tsx ChevronUp/ChevronDown have aria-label 'Move indicator up/down' and disabled at edges (lines 121-137). But the on-chart legend's eye/gear/x cluster is hover-only: `className="... opacity-0 transition-opacity group-hover:opacity-100"` (indicator-legend.tsx:36) with no group-focus-within (grep confirms zero focus-within usage in the terminal feature), so a keyboard user tabbing in can't see/reach them reliably. real.notes: Verified real - no group-focus-within anywhere, and line 36 is hover-only. real.title: No way to create an alert from an indicator — legend has no overflow/Add-alert entry point

Props interface has only those 3 callbacks — no onAddAlert, no overflow. chart-pane.tsx:1418-1427 wires them: onToggleVisible→updateIndicator visible toggle, onSettings→requestIndicatorSettings(id), onRemove→removeIndicator(pane.id,id). In chart-pane.tsx, pass `onAddAlert` next to onSettings/onRemove at line 1425-1426, calling a new store action / dialog opener seeded with `pane.classicIndicators.find(i=>i.id===id)`. real.scBehavior: packages/types/src/alerts.ts:108 declares `type: 'ma_cross'` (single literal). routes/alerts.ts:73 `alertCreateSchema` uses `type: z.literal('ma_cross')`; `alertUpdateSchema` (78-83) has no type field but config is `maCrossConfigSchema`. alert-engine.ts load() line 86 `if (row.type !== 'ma_cross') continue;` and evaluate() line 223 only ever calls `computeMaCross`. The `SignalCondition` union (trading.ts:94-117) DOES define indicator_compare/price_crosses, but the alert engine cannot run them.

Make routes/alerts.ts `alertCreateSchema` (line 70) a `z.discriminatedUnion('type', [maCrossSchema, indicatorSchema])`. In alert-engine.ts, branch load() (the `continue` at line 86 must instead build an indicator AlertDefinition) and evaluate() (line 199) on `alert.type`, keeping `computeMaCross` verbatim for ma_cross. CRITICAL constraint correctly called out: the 48 live ma_cross rows must keep loading — guard so a missing/legacy `type` defaults to 'ma_cross'. rowToAlert() (alerts.ts:122-138) also hard-casts `type as 'ma_cross'` and must branch too.

real.title: Working plot-cross condition evaluator already exists but is trapped in the MT5 SignalRunner
real.scBehavior: mt5/signal-runner.ts defines `evaluateCondition` (lines 102-177) and `evaluateConditions` (82-100) and `indicatorRefs` (66-80) as CLOSURES inside `createSignalRunner` — not exported. They fully implement indicator_compare + price_crosses with >,<,>=,<=,==,crosses_above,crosses_below, plot-vs-constant/plot/price, using `computeIndicatorChannel`/`setIndicatorMetadata` from @supercharts/indicators/runner. real.title: Create-alert form has no plot/operator/level picker — it is a pure MA-cross form
real.scBehavior: CreateAlertForm (alerts-dialog.tsx:2138-2272) only renders MA type/length/source/labels/web+telegram toggles, builds a MaCrossAlertConfig, and POSTs `type: 'ma_cross'` (line 2169). The full plot/operator/RHS UI EXISTS in signal-builder-dialog.tsx: `IndicatorCompareRow` (lines 287-412) and `PriceCrossesRow` (414-504) — channel dropdown from `spec.channels` via INDICATOR_LOOKUP, operator dropdown incl. crosses_above/below, constant/indicator/price RHS toggle. real.title: Indicator instance ids are per-pane/client-only — alert engine has no inputs to compute the plot server-side
real.scBehavior: runner.ts computeIndicatorChannel() (lines 41-63): `const meta = activeMeta?.byId.get(ref.id)` then `const type = (meta?.type ?? ref.id).toLowerCase()` and inputs default to registry defaults via `spec.inputs.map(i=>[i.key, inputs[i.key] ?? i.default])`. Without setIndicatorMetadata, EMA falls back to length=21 (runner.ts:78). signal-runner.ts handles this by calling setIndicatorMetadata(recipe.indicatorSpecs) (line 92-94); the bulk-subscribe flow persists indicatorSpecs on the recipe. But legend instances live in the per-pane Zustand store / indicator_layouts; an alert row keyed by id alone would lose the inputs.
real.fixRefined: When building the `indicator` alert body in alerts-dialog.tsx, copy the full IndicatorInstance (id+type+name+inputs) from pane.classicIndicators into the alert config (e.g. `indicatorSpec`/`indicatorSpecs`). In alert-engine.ts's indicator branch, call `setIndicatorMetadata([config.indicatorSpec])` before evaluating — mirroring signal-runner.ts:92. Reuses the existing metadata mechanism; no new math. IndicatorInstance (chart.ts:89) carries id/type/name/inputs, so the copy is complete.
real.notes: Verified end-to-end against runner.ts and the signal-runner precedent. Medium is fair — it is a correctness requirement for the indicator-alert feature, but only matters once ALERTFROMIND-2/4 exist. setIndicatorMetadata uses a module-level `activeMeta`, so the call must happen per-evaluation pass right before computeIndicatorChannel (as signal-runner does), or concurrent alerts on different specs could race; worth flagging in the fix.

real.title: AlertEvent shape assumes maValue — indicator alerts would have nothing meaningful to log/deliver
real.scBehavior: AlertEvent (alerts.ts:131) has required `maValue: number`. The INSERT (alert-engine.ts:268-290) writes ma_value, and the DB column is `ma_value REAL NOT NULL` (db.ts:195). formatTelegramMessage (alert-engine.ts:449-471) renders MA-specific copy ('EMA(20) on close'). EventHistory (alerts-dialog.tsx:2423) prints `MA {formatPrice(e.maValue)}`. None of this describes a generic plot cross.
real.fixRefined: Make `maValue` optional in the AlertEvent TS type and add optional `conditionLabel?: string` + `indicatorValue?: number`. CRITICAL REFINEMENT (candidate missed this): `ma_value` is NOT NULL in the SQLite schema (db.ts:195), so the indicator branch CANNOT insert NULL — either ALTER the column to add `DEFAULT 0` / make it nullable via a migration, or have the indicator branch write the indicator's plot value into ma_value (preferred: reuse the column to store `indicatorValue`). In alert-engine.ts branch the event build + formatTelegramMessage on alert.type; for indicator, derive conditionLabel from the SignalCondition (reuse describeCondition in apps/web/lib/strategy-describe.ts, or a small server mirror — note that file is in the web package, so a server-side copy/move is needed). Guard EventHistory (alerts-dialog.tsx:2423) to show conditionLabel when the alert is an indicator type.
real.notes: REAL but the candidate's fix is incomplete: it says 'Additive columns only — no migration of existing rows', yet ma_value is NOT NULL (db.ts:195) so an indicator event cannot omit it without a schema change or writing a real value. Also `describeCondition` lives in the WEB package (apps/web/lib/strategy-describe.ts) — alert-engine.ts is server-side and cannot import it; needs a server mirror or shared package. Added db.ts to files touched. Severity medium is fair.

real.title: Backtest/optimize/sizer/walk-forward actions on an indicator alert would 400 — they assume ma_cross
real.scBehavior: AlertRow (alerts-dialog.tsx:1040-1260) renders Backtest (Activity), Optimize (Sliders), Walk-forward (Shuffle), Paper (ClipboardList), Sizer (Calculator) buttons unconditionally for every alert. real.fixRefined: In AlertRow (alerts-dialog.tsx:1189-1254), gate the Backtest/Optimize/Walk-forward/Sizer buttons behind `alert.type === 'ma_cross'` (hide, or disable with a tooltip) for indicator alerts. real.title: Floating OHLC tooltip chases the cursor instead of living in a fixed status line
real.scBehavior: TooltipLayer (packages/chart-core/src/layers/tooltip.ts) is registered by default in registerDefaultLayers() (chart-core.ts line 299) with zIndex 97 and visible=true. render() draws a rounded O/H/L/C/V/Δ panel at x=crosshair.x+14, y=crosshair.y+14 (tooltip.ts lines 39-40), flipping sides only to avoid clipping the axis. It moves with the cursor and overlays candles.
real.tvBehavior: TradingView shows OHLCV in a fixed top-left status line next to the symbol; values update in place and never occlude price action with a moving box.
real.gap: SC draws a cursor-tracking OHLC box over the candles where TV shows OHLC once in a fixed status line. The box occludes price action as the mouse moves.
real.fixRefined: Surface OHLCV in the existing fixed UI instead of the floating canvas box. The hovered candle index is already computed as `legendHoverIdx` (chart-pane.tsx line 1344) and `hoverTime` is tracked (line 149). Extend `IndicatorLegend` (indicator-legend.tsx) with an optional OHLC row (extend the `LegendRow`/Props contract in indicator-legend-util.ts which currently only carries id/name/color/summary/value/visible at line 58), and either default `TooltipLayer.visible=false` or gate it behind a per-pane `overlays.cursorTooltip` flag toggled via the existing togglePaneOverlay. Keep the candle-column highlight strip (tooltip.ts lines 81-87) by moving it into CrosshairLayer.render. Note: the top-bar header (chart-pane.tsx lines 1405-1410) shows only last price + percent, NOT full OHLC, so OHLC is not strictly duplicated today — the real problem is occlusion, not duplication.
real.notes: Real. Downgraded high→medium: this is a noticeable UX deviation from TV, not a missing core capability — full OHLC IS available (just in a moving box). The candidate's 'OHLC is duplicated in the legend' claim is overstated: IndicatorLegend renders indicator values, and the top-bar header shows only price/percent, so OHLC currently appears only in the floating box.

real.title: Right-click menu is overlay-toggle-only — missing every TV staple action
real.scBehavior: ChartContextMenu (chart-pane.tsx lines 1488-1553) renders only: Reset zoom, Delete selected drawing, and Show/Hide for heatmap/volume profile/deep trades/footprint/volume pane. onContextMenu in chart-core.ts (lines 474-491) already computes time and price under the cursor and threads them through onPointerEvent, but the chart-pane handler discards them: line 160 stores only `{ x: e.x, y: e.y }`, and the menu state type at line 115 is `{ x: number; y: number } | null`.
real.tvBehavior: TV's chart context menu offers Add indicator, Add alert at the clicked price, Reset chart, Chart/scale settings, Copy price, Object tree, Hide all drawings — and captures the price/time under the cursor so 'Add alert' and 'Copy price' act on that exact level.
real.gap: None of TV's core context actions exist; the menu is purely overlay toggles, and the cursor price/time (already available) is thrown away.
real.fixRefined: In chart-pane.tsx line 160 store `{ x: e.x, y: e.y, price: e.price, time: e.time }` and widen the `menu` state type at line 115 to include price/time. Extend ChartContextMenu with a top section: 'Copy price' (`navigator.clipboard.writeText(formatPrice(menu.price))` — formatPrice is already imported at chart-pane.tsx line 71), 'Add indicator' (open the existing indicators dialog via the store), 'Reset chart' (reuse the existing onResetZoom wiring at line 1453, relabel), and 'Add alert at <price>' that opens the EXISTING alert-create UI prefilled with this symbol/interval/price. Do NOT auto-create or mutate the 48 live alerts — only open the creation UI prefilled. Keep overlay toggles in a lower section.
real.notes: Real and accurately scoped. Severity high is fair — these are core TV context actions users expect. Fix correctly respects the live-alerts constraint (open prefilled UI only, no auto-create).

real.title: No log / percent / auto price-scale modes and no price-axis context menu
real.scBehavior: PriceScale (scale.ts) defines `mode: 'linear' | 'log' | 'percent'` (line 77/86) and implements correct LOG math in priceToY (lines 100-104) and yToPrice (lines 116-119). Mode is hardcoded `mode: 'linear'` in the ChartCore constructor (chart-core.ts line 118); there is no setter, fitPriceScaleToVisible always calls the linear fit() (lines 531-542), and onContextMenu fires the same generic menu for axis-pane clicks (it never distinguishes `x >= geometry.axisPane.x`, which is only used for priceAxisDrag at line 394).
real.tvBehavior: Right-clicking the price axis opens a dedicated menu (Auto/fit, Logarithmic, Percent, Regular, Scale settings) with small Auto/Log/% toggles at the axis. Log scale is essential for assets moving orders of magnitude.
real.gap: Log scale math exists but is unreachable dead code (no setter, no UI, no axis menu). Percent mode is declared in the type but NOT actually implemented in the math.
real.fixRefined: Add `ChartCore.setPriceScaleMode(mode)` that sets `this.priceScale.state.mode`, re-runs fitPriceScaleToVisible(), and markDirty(). In chart-core.ts onContextMenu (line 474), detect `x >= this.geometry.axisPane.x` and emit a distinct event (e.g. add a `target: 'scale'` field to ChartPointerEvent, or a new type) so chart-pane.tsx can render a scale-specific menu (Auto / Logarithmic / Regular). IMPORTANT CORRECTION: 'percent' is NOT implemented — priceToY/yToPrice only branch on 'log' and otherwise fall through to the linear path, so percent currently behaves identically to linear. Shipping a working 'Percent' option requires ADDING percent math (normalize against the first visible close) to priceToY/yToPrice and fit() — it is not just 'wiring existing math.' Log mode, by contrast, is genuinely complete and only needs wiring.
real.notes: Real. Candidate's core claim (log is working dead code, no toggle, no axis menu) is correct. But its assertion that 'the math path already supports' percent is WRONG — only log is implemented; percent falls through to linear. Refined the fix to add percent math rather than just wire it. Effort L stands given the new event-routing + axis menu + percent math.

real.title: Pan and zoom are instant/1:1 — no inertia or smooth zoom, feels abrupt vs TV
real.scBehavior: Pan is a direct per-event 1:1 translation: onPointerMove calls timeScale.pan(x-lastX) / priceScale.pan(y-lastY) (chart-core.ts lines 407-408); TimeScale.pan just does `rightTime -= dxPixels / pxPerMs` (scale.ts line 61). Wheel zoom applies a fixed factor 1.15 per tick instantly (chart-core.ts line 380). The render loop is a dirty-flag RAF that only repaints on markDirty() (lines 556-562); there is no continuous animation channel, so motion halts the instant the pointer/wheel stops. No velocity/momentum/inertia/decay symbols exist anywhere in chart-core/src.
real.tvBehavior: TV pans with momentum (a fast flick glides and decelerates) and zooms with a brief smoothed ramp, a large part of why it 'feels smooth.'
real.gap: No flick-pan momentum and no eased zoom; movement stops immediately, reading as less polished than TV.
real.fixRefined: Add an optional momentum pass to the existing `loop` (chart-core.ts lines 556-562) without rebuilding it. Track pointer velocity across the last 2-3 pointermove deltas in attachInteractions; on pointerup while dragging, if speed exceeds a threshold, start a decay that markDirty()s each frame and applies timeScale.pan(vx) with vx*=~0.92 until below ~0.1px, cancelling on the next pointerdown/wheel (note: the RAF loop currently early-returns when !dirty, so the decay must keep setting dirty=true each frame). Gate behind a ChartCoreOptions flag so tests stay deterministic. Pure additive; no scale-math change.
real.notes: Real and accurately described. Downgraded medium→low: this is genuine polish/feel, not a missing capability — pan/zoom work correctly today, they just lack easing. Honest severity per the rubric (nice-to-have).

real.title: Canvas has no cursor affordances (no crosshair / grab / grabbing)
real.scBehavior: The canvas JSX is `className="absolute inset-0 h-full w-full"` (chart-pane.tsx line 1416) with no cursor style, and attachInteractions (chart-core.ts lines 368-509) never mutates canvas.style.cursor. grep for 'cursor' in chart-core/src returns only two doc-comment hits (scale.ts lines 57, 66) and chart-pane.tsx has no style.cursor or cursor-* class. So the OS arrow shows everywhere.
real.tvBehavior: The chart cursor communicates state: crosshair over the plot, grab/grabbing while panning, resize cursor over the axes, target/pencil while a draw tool is armed.
real.fixRefined: In attachInteractions (chart-core.ts), set `cvs.style.cursor` from the pointer handlers: 'crosshair' on hover inside the price pane (onPointerMove hover branch ~line 419), 'grabbing' on pointerdown / while dragging (line 388/406), 'ns-resize' when priceAxisDrag (x >= axisPane.x, line 394), 'ew-resize' for timeAxisDrag (line 395), resetting on pointerup/pointerleave. For the armed-drawing-tool state, set 'crosshair' or 'copy' when shouldSuppressPan() returns true. A handful of one-line style assignments; no layout impact.
real.notes: Real, fully confirmed by grep. Low severity is correct (affordance polish). Fix lands in the right handlers.

real.title: Crosshair does not magnet-snap to OHLC and price label is raw cursor price
real.scBehavior: The crosshair price comes straight from the cursor y: onPointerMove sets `crosshair = { x, y, time, price: this.priceScale.yToPrice(y) }` (chart-core.ts lines 423-424). AxisLayer prints formatPrice(crosshair.price) from that raw value (axis.ts lines 66, 74). CrosshairLayer draws the horizontal line at the raw crosshair.y (crosshair.ts lines 39-40). There is no snapping; no 'magnet'/'snap' symbol exists in chart-core/src. findCandleAt already locates the hovered candle in tooltip.ts (lines 93-111) but is private to that layer.
real.tvBehavior: In Magnet mode TV snaps the crosshair to the nearest OHLC level of the hovered bar; the axis price label and horizontal line reflect that snapped value.
real.gap: No magnet/snap; the crosshair price label is an arbitrary cursor value rather than a real OHLC level.
real.fixRefined: Factor findCandleAt out of tooltip.ts into a shared exported helper (e.g. in a scale/util module so both TooltipLayer and ChartCore can import it without duplicating the binary search). In chart-core.ts onPointerMove, when an optional `magnet` flag is on, after computing time/price find the hovered candle and snap crosshair.y/crosshair.price to the nearest of its open/high/low/close (via priceScale.priceToY for the snapped y). AxisLayer and CrosshairLayer then render the snapped value automatically. Expose a per-chart magnet toggle, default OFF so current behavior is unchanged. No new math.
real.notes: Real. Low severity correct (opt-in precision aid). The only refinement: findCandleAt is currently a private function in tooltip.ts, so the fix must export/relocate it rather than call it cross-module.

real.title: Indicators not searchable by acronym/alias ("EMA", "BB", "RSI", "DMI" fail)
real.scBehavior: Confirmed. In indicators-dialog.tsx the matches() predicate (lines 254-258) tests only e.label, e.desc and the group name as lowercase substrings. The EMA catalog row is label "Exponential MA" / desc "Exponential moving average." / group "Moving Averages" — none contain the substring "ema". Keltner's desc "EMA ± ATR channels" (line 124) lowercases to "ema ± atr channels", which DOES contain "ema", so typing "EMA" returns ONLY Keltner Channels — exactly as the brief captured. "BB"/"BBands" never hit "Bollinger Bands"/"bollinger" (no adjacent "bb"); "DMI" never hits "ADX / DMI" (its desc/label both lack "dmi") wait— label is "ADX / DMI" which DOES contain "dmi", but "%R"/"WPR" miss Williams, "STOCH" misses Stochastic, "KC"/"DC"/"SAR"/"ST" all miss. No aliases?: string[] field exists anywhere in registry.ts (verified by grep).
real.tvBehavior: TradingView indexes every study by full name AND acronym; typing EMA/BB/MACD/ATR/RSI jumps straight to the match. Acronym search is the primary power-user add path.
real.gap: No acronym/alias/synonym index in either search path. The headline "EMA"->only-Keltner symptom is real, plus BB->Bollinger, %R->Williams, KC->Keltner, SAR->Parabolic SAR, ST->Supertrend, STOCH->Stochastic, AVWAP/VWAP-sigma are unreachable by the names traders type.
real.fixRefined: Add optional `aliases?: string[]` to `IndicatorSpec` in registry.ts (alongside `description` at line 35) and populate per entry (e.g. bollinger:['bb','bbands'], williams_r:['%r','wpr'], stochastic:['stoch'], keltner:['kc'], donchian:['dc'], psar:['sar'], supertrend:['st'], cci/mfi/obv/cmf/vwap/rsi/macd/atr/adx as their own acronyms). In indicators-dialog.tsx, resolve each classic Entry's aliases via INDICATOR_LOOKUP[e.type]?.aliases (already imported, line 11) and extend matches() (line 254) with `(aliases?.some(a => a.includes(lower) || lower.includes(a)) ?? false)`. Mirror the same alias lookup in indicator-panel.tsx IndicatorPickerList filter (lines 191-193). NOTE: the right-rail picker already lowercases the query (line 190) and matches s.type, so "EMA" DOES find EMA there today — the candidate's claim that the right rail misses "EMA" unless typed lowercase is WRONG; the real right-rail gap is acronyms that aren't the type string (BB, %R, KC, etc.). The dialog is the one with the headline "EMA"->only-Keltner failure.
real.notes: Core gap is real and high-value (matches the live brief verbatim). Refined: corrected the candidate's inaccurate right-rail "EMA misses" claim (query is lowercased at line 190), and noted "DMI" actually already matches because the catalog label is literally "ADX / DMI". Severity stays high — acronym search is the primary add path and the EMA case is a genuine dead-end.

real.title: No unified Moving Average entry with MA-type dropdown; DEMA/TEMA coded but unreachable; VWMA missing
real.scBehavior: Confirmed on all points. registry.ts has four separate MA entries: sma(39-50), ema(51-61), wma(62-69), hma(70-77); no `maType` enum input exists anywhere. ma.ts exports working dema(96-105), tema(107-117) and rma/Wilder(119-133). runner.ts has live `case 'dema'`(92) and `case 'tema'`(97) branches — but neither dema nor tema has a registry entry, so both are unreachable from the UI (the dialog CATALOG Moving Averages group lists only sma/ema/wma/hma, lines 113-116). vwma confirmed absent from volume.ts (grep: only vwap/obv/cmf/volumeOscillator/rvol/vwapBands/initialBalance exported) and has no runner case.
real.tvBehavior: TradingView ships one "Moving Average" study with a TYPE dropdown (SMA/EMA/WMA/RMA/VWMA + DEMA/TEMA) that recomputes in place, rather than a study per flavour.
real.gap: Users can't switch MA type in place, and two already-computed types (DEMA, TEMA) plus VWMA/RMA are absent from the picker despite the math existing (DEMA/TEMA even have runner cases wired).
real.fixRefined: Lowest-risk additive path: add `dema` and `tema` IndicatorSpec entries to INDICATOR_REGISTRY (mirror the `ema` entry at lines 51-61: length int default 21 + sourceInput(), overlay) — zero runner work since cases 'dema'/'tema' already exist (runner.ts:92-101) — and add both to the Moving Averages group in indicators-dialog.tsx CATALOG (lines 111-118). For VWMA, add `vwma(values, volumes, length)` to ma.ts and a `case 'vwma'` in runner.ts reading candle.volume (no fabricated data). Optionally a true TV-style single `moving_average` spec with a maType enum dispatching in one runner case to the existing ma.ts fns, keeping the four legacy entries intact.
real.notes: Real. DEMA/TEMA being computable-but-unreachable is the most concrete, lowest-effort sub-part (S on its own — just two registry rows + two catalog rows, no math/runner change). VWMA is the only piece needing new math. Severity medium is fair: it's missing coverage, not broken behavior.

real.title: No Offset input on MAs/overlays
real.scBehavior: Confirmed. grep for "offset" across packages/indicators/src returns nothing. No registry entry has an offset input; neither priceFromCandle/pricesFromCandles (ma.ts:5-21) nor the runner MA cases (runner.ts:72-101) apply any index shift. Output arrays are strictly 1:1 with candles, so there is no plumbing to displace a series.
real.tvBehavior: Every TradingView MA (and most overlays) exposes an Offset input shifting the plotted line N bars left/right — standard for displaced MAs and forward projection.
real.gap: Displaced moving averages are impossible; experienced traders expect Offset on any MA.
real.fixRefined: Add an `offsetInput()` helper in registry.ts (type 'int', default 0, min -500, max 500) alongside sourceInput() (line 355) and include it on the sma/ema/wma/hma specs (and any new MA entry). In runner.ts add a small shiftSeries(values, offset) post-step in the MA case branches (lines 72-101) using numberInput(inputs.offset, 0). Default 0 preserves today's output. Confine to overlay MAs first; the chart layer already plots index-aligned arrays so a pre-shifted array works.
real.notes: Real but genuinely a nice-to-have for most users; low is the right severity (candidate already had it low). Effort M is slightly generous — adding the input + a shift helper is closer to S, but threading it through all MA cases and verifying right-shift padding behavior justifies M.

real.title: Input metadata incomplete/inconsistent — missing min/max on most numeric inputs; no per-input tooltip
real.scBehavior: Confirmed. IndicatorInputSpec (registry.ts:9-18) has min/max/step but NO tooltip/description field (grep confirms the only "tooltip" in src is the comment on the indicator-level `description` at line 34). Bounds are uneven: only sma.length and ema.length carry min:1,max:500 (lines 45,57); wma.length/hma.length/bollinger.length/keltner.*/adx.length/atr.length/cci/mfi/williams_r/stochastic periods have NO min/max/step; rsi.length has min:2 but no max (line 84). The editor (indicator-panel.tsx:288-300) passes input.min/input.max straight to the number input, so absent bounds = unbounded field; numberInput in runner.ts (288-295) does NOT clamp, so length 0/negative yields a blank/garbage series. Note: the editor DOES already supply a step fallback (line 290: input.step ?? (float?0.1:1)), so the 'no step' part is mitigated in the UI even where the spec omits step.
real.tvBehavior: TradingView inputs carry sensible min/max/step plus a hover tooltip per input; the settings dialog enforces bounds and shows help text.
real.gap: Inconsistent/absent bounds let users enter invalid params (length 0/negative -> empty series); no per-input help. A noticeable quality gap vs TV's guided inputs.
real.fixRefined: In registry.ts: (1) add optional `tooltip?: string` to IndicatorInputSpec (lines 9-18) and populate it for common inputs (Length, Source, Multiplier, Fast/Slow/Signal); (2) backfill min/max/step on every numeric input lacking them (lengths min 1, multipliers min 0.1 step 0.1, periods min 1). In indicator-panel.tsx IndicatorEditor (lines 283-302) render input.tooltip as a title=/help line and clamp onChange to min/max before calling onChange (line 296). Optionally add a min-floor in runner.ts numberInput (line 288). Pure metadata + small UI tweak; touches no working math or defaults.
real.notes: Real. Minor correction to the candidate: the editor already has a step fallback, so the practical gap is min/max bounds + tooltips, not step. Severity medium is defensible because unbounded length lets a user silently blank an indicator; could be argued low since most users never type 0, but the no-clamp + blank-series outcome is a real correctness/UX rough edge.

real.title: Source input missing on Bollinger (TV exposes it)
real.scBehavior: Confirmed. sourceInput() (registry.ts:355-371) is attached only to sma/ema/wma/hma/rsi/macd/roc. The bollinger spec (registry.ts:168-177) has NO source input. The runner's case 'bollinger' (runner.ts:156-168) passes only length+multiplier, and bollinger() in volatility.ts hardcodes the series via `const closes = candles.map((c) => c.close)` (line 48) with no source option. So Bollinger cannot be computed on hl2/typical price.
real.tvBehavior: TradingView Bollinger Bands defaults to close but is source-selectable (close/open/hl2/hlc3/ohlc4...). Single-series studies expose a Source dropdown.
real.gap: Bollinger (the most-used band study) can't be computed on hl2/typical price as TV allows. Minor but a real coverage gap.
real.fixRefined: Add sourceInput() to the bollinger spec (registry.ts:172). Thread `source: (inputs.source as PriceSource) ?? 'close'` through case 'bollinger' (runner.ts:156). Extend BollingerOptions (volatility.ts:32-35) with optional `source?: PriceSource` and replace the hardcoded `candles.map((c) => c.close)` (line 48) with pricesFromCandles(candles, opts.source ?? 'close') (already importable from ./ma). Default 'close' preserves current output. Scope to Bollinger only — leave HLC-defined studies (CCI/Williams/MFI) without Source, matching TV.
real.notes: Real and cleanly additive; the existing pricesFromCandles helper (ma.ts:17) makes the volatility.ts change trivial. Low severity is correct — it's a single-study refinement. Candidate's mention of 'Stochastic legitimately takes a source on TV' is debatable (TV Stochastic is HLC-based and does not expose Source), so the fix is correctly scoped to Bollinger only.
